meaning: F8E1 appears when the tub does not reach the expected water level within about ten minutes. The ACU sees valves open but the pressure switch never reports a rise—one of the most common Maytag top-load codes.
causes: Closed or partially closed wall supply valves
Clogged inlet screens at the washer connection
Drain hose siphoning water out as fast as it fills
Weak or failed water inlet valve solenoids
fixes: Turn both hot and cold valves fully counterclockwise behind the washer
Disconnect fill hoses and rinse the mesh screens into a bucket
Confirm the drain hose has an air gap and is not sealed in the standpipe
Start a fill and watch through the lid for incoming water within one minute
when_to_call_technician: Strong flow at disconnected hoses with an empty tub after ten minutes points to inlet valve or pressure system replacement.

title: Maytag Washing Machine F9E1 Error Code
summary: Long drain—water not leaving the tub
meta_description: Maytag F9E1 drain pump fault. Check drain hose, coin trap, standpipe height, and pump operation.
meaning: F9E1 means the drain pump ran but the water level did not drop within the allowed window—typically eight to ten minutes. Maytag stops the cycle to prevent walking the machine with a full tub.
causes: Kinked drain hose pinned against the wall
Coin trap or pump filter packed with lint and coins
Standpipe height above 96 inches creating back pressure
Failed drain pump motor humming without moving water
fixes: Disconnect the drain hose at the standpipe and drain into a bucket during a test cycle
Access and clean the pump filter after unplugging the washer
Straighten the drain hose and keep proper standpipe insertion depth
Listen for pump hum at drain—silence with voltage present means a new pump
when_to_call_technician: F9E1 after a cleared filter and free-flowing standpipe usually requires drain pump replacement.

title: Maytag Dryer F01 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag dryer F01 means EEPROM error on the main control. Unplug, reseat harnesses, replace board if fault returns.
meaning: F01 on Maytag dryers signals the main control board failed its internal memory check. Heat, motor, and moisture sensing commands stop until the board resets or is replaced.
causes: Lightning surge through the laundry circuit
Loose main harness connector after moving the dryer
Moisture corrosion on the board from a vent leak
Failed EEPROM on the electronic control assembly
fixes: Unplug the dryer for five minutes before restoring power
Remove the top panel and reseat the large harness into the control board
Inspect for burnt traces near the line input terminals
Run a timed dry on air fluff as a board function test
when_to_call_technician: F01 that returns immediately after power-up requires a new Maytag electronic control matched to your model suffix.

title: Maytag Dryer F02 Error Code
summary: Key stuck on the user interface
meta_description: Maytag F02 stuck button on dryer console. Clean the panel, power-cycle, replace UI if keys read pressed constantly.
meaning: F02 means the console detects a keypad or touch button held continuously. The dryer will not start a cycle while it thinks a selection key is pressed.
causes: Static discharge locking a touch pad trace
Spilled liquid on the console membrane
Cracked overlay shorting adjacent pads
Failed user interface board
fixes: Wipe the console dry with a soft cloth
Unplug two minutes and touch each button once after restart
Avoid leaning on the console during operation
Replace the UI if F02 shows with no keys touched
when_to_call_technician: UI board replacement is straightforward but requires matching the part to your console style—order by model number.

title: Maytag Dryer F03 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F03 exhaust temperature sensor fault. Check thermistor wiring at the blower housing.
meaning: F03 means the exhaust thermistor sent an open-circuit reading. The control cannot modulate heat safely without exhaust temperature feedback and may shut off the burner or element.
causes: Two-wire thermistor connector loose at the blower housing
Broken wire in the harness near the drum bulkhead
Failed thermistor element internally open
Heavy lint shorting the sensor connector
fixes: Unplug and reseat the thermistor plug on the blower outlet
Vacuum lint from the connector area
Measure thermistor resistance against the service chart at room temperature
Run a timed cycle on low heat after wiring is verified
when_to_call_technician: Open thermistor at room temp with intact wiring requires sensor or harness replacement.

title: Maytag Dryer F04 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F04 moisture sensing bar fault. Clean sensor strips inside drum, check wiring to the board.
meaning: F04 indicates the moisture sensor circuit is open. Auto-dry cycles rely on these metal strips inside the front bulkhead to detect dampness and end the cycle.
causes: Sensor wire unplugged behind the front panel
Coating of fabric softener insulating the bars
Corroded sensor terminals from years of use
Open circuit in the sensor harness
fixes: Clean the two metal moisture bars inside the drum with rubbing alcohol
Remove front bulkhead access and verify the sensor harness is plugged in
Inspect for broken wires where the door harness flexes
Run Sensor Dry on a small damp load as a functional test
when_to_call_technician: Persistent F04 with clean bars and seated wiring needs moisture sensor harness or board service.

title: Maytag Dryer F05 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F05 door switch fault. Confirm door clicks closed, test switch continuity, replace if the drum won't run with door shut.
meaning: F05 means the door switch never reported closed. Maytag dryers block motor and heat circuits until the door strike depresses the switch plunger fully.
causes: Worn door catch not pressing the switch
Broken door switch lever
Door hinge sag misaligning the strike
Failed door switch internally open
fixes: Close the door firmly and listen for the drum light to extinguish
Inspect the switch plunger through the front panel gap
Replace the door switch if the drum won't turn with the door fully shut
Check hinge screws for looseness causing misalignment
when_to_call_technician: Door switch replacement is a common DIY repair—call service only if wiring to the switch is damaged inside the door.

title: Maytag Dryer F06 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F06 UI-to-control communication error. Reseat console harness, inspect for corrosion.
meaning: F06 means the user interface board and main control lost serial communication. Display, knob input, and heat commands cannot synchronize.
causes: Loose ribbon harness between console and main board
Power surge damaging communication chips
Corrosion on the 6-pin UI connector
Failed UI or main control not acknowledging signals
fixes: Unplug and remove the console to reseat the UI harness
Inspect ribbon cable for creases or pin damage
Power-cycle five minutes after reconnecting
Test with a basic timed dry cycle
when_to_call_technician: F06 after reseating both ends of the UI harness usually requires UI board replacement first.

title: Maytag Dryer F07 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F07 shorted moisture sensor. Clean bars, dry the harness, replace sensor strip if short persists.
meaning: F07 is the opposite of F04—the moisture sensor circuit reads a short. Auto-dry may end immediately or not advance because the board sees constant wetness.
causes: Metal debris bridging the two sensor bars
Water splash on the sensor harness during cleaning
Shorted wires where harness rubs the cabinet
Failed sensor strip with internal short
fixes: Clean and dry the moisture bars inside the drum
Inspect the harness behind the bulkhead for pinched insulation
Disconnect the sensor plug and measure for short to ground
Run a timed dry instead of auto-dry until repaired
when_to_call_technician: Open sensor circuit tests good but F07 remains with the harness disconnected—replace the sensor assembly.

title: Maytag Dryer F08 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F08 static on user interface. Touch Start again after discharge; add humidity if laundry room is very dry.
meaning: F08 is a static-sensitive keypad fault. Dry winter air or synthetic clothing can discharge into the touch panel and momentarily confuse the UI.
causes: Very low humidity in the laundry room
Static buildup from synthetic loads
Ungrounded outlet missing equipment ground
Sensitive UI board without discharge protection
fixes: Touch a grounded metal surface before pressing dryer buttons
Unplug one minute and retry the cycle selection
Avoid dragging feet on carpet before using the console
Install a humidifier nearby if F08 is frequent in winter
when_to_call_technician: F08 on every start with a verified grounded outlet may need UI board replacement.

title: Maytag Dryer F09 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F09 incompatible software between UI and control. Both boards may need matched replacement pair.
meaning: F09 appears when the user interface and main control report mismatched software versions. After a partial board replacement the two assemblies may not communicate correctly.
causes: UI board replaced without matching main control revision
Main control replaced with incompatible firmware level
Factory flash corruption on one board
Aftermarket control not paired to the console
fixes: Record model and serial numbers from the rating plate
Verify both boards carry the same engineering revision suffix
Power-cycle after any single-board swap
Contact Maytag parts with the serial to order a matched pair
when_to_call_technician: F09 after replacing either board usually requires installing the manufacturer-matched UI and control set together.

title: Maytag Dryer F10 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F10 power supply fault. Test the outlet for 120V, check breaker and cord terminal block.
meaning: F10 means the control detected line voltage outside the safe 108–132 VAC window. Motor and heat outputs are disabled to protect components.
causes: Loose cord at the terminal block
Shared circuit with heavy loads causing voltage sag
Tripped or weak breaker contact
Incorrect outlet wiring with lost neutral
fixes: Test the outlet with a multimeter under load
Tighten the power cord strain relief and terminal screws
Move the dryer to a dedicated 20-amp laundry circuit if possible
Reset the breaker fully off then on
when_to_call_technician: Voltage below 108 VAC at the terminal block with a good outlet indicates house wiring service, not the dryer.

title: Maytag Dryer F11 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F11 shorted key on dryer console. Dry the panel, replace UI if a key reads shorted without touch.
meaning: F11 means the UI sees a continuous short on one keypad line—different from F02 stuck key which is a logic state. Liquid spills or board failure are common triggers.
causes: Water from a nearby sink splashed on the console
Shorted membrane under the Start pad
Liquid fabric softener on the touch surface
Failed UI board with internal short
fixes: Power off and dry the console thoroughly for several hours
Inspect for visible moisture under the plastic overlay
Unplug and leave the door open to ventilate the control area
Replace the UI if F11 persists on a dry panel
when_to_call_technician: UI replacement is the typical fix when drying does not clear F11.

title: Maytag Dryer F12 Error Code
summary: Relay failure on the main control
meta_description: Maytag F12 bad relay on electronic control. Heat or motor may not energize—board replacement likely.
meaning: F12 indicates a drive relay on the main control failed its self-test. The board cannot reliably switch heat, motor, or valve outputs.
causes: Relay welded closed from age or surge
Burnt relay driver transistor on the board
Moisture intrusion on the relay bank
Overloaded motor circuit stressing the relay
fixes: Unplug five minutes and attempt one timed dry cycle
Listen for heat or drum start—silence with F12 confirms output failure
Inspect the board for burnt relay areas near heat outputs
Note model number before ordering the control assembly
when_to_call_technician: F12 almost always requires main electronic control replacement—relays are not field-serviceable.

title: Maytag Dryer F13 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F13 control memory checksum fault. Power-cycle; replace main board if code returns every start.
meaning: F13 means the main control EEPROM failed a checksum verification at power-up. Stored cycle profiles and calibration data may be corrupt.
causes: Brownout during a previous cycle
Incomplete power loss leaving memory half-written
Age-related EEPROM degradation
Static discharge at the board connector
fixes: Unplug for ten minutes to allow full capacitor discharge
Reseat the main harness at the control board
Avoid using extension cords that cause voltage dips
Run one short air-fluff cycle after reset
when_to_call_technician: Recurring F13 at every power-up requires a new main control—memory cannot be reflashed in the field.

title: Maytag Dryer F14 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F14 alternate memory fault on dryer control. Treat like F13—inspect harness, replace board.
meaning: F14 is a second checksum fault path on the same control platform as F13. The board detected corrupted non-volatile memory during initialization.
causes: Power surge through the neutral leg
Water drip on the board from a roof leak above
Connector intermittently losing 5V logic supply
Manufacturing defect in a batch EEPROM
fixes: Verify the dryer cord and outlet ground are intact
Inspect the control area for water stains or pest damage
Reseat all multi-pin connectors on the board
Document the code and serial for warranty if applicable
when_to_call_technician: F14 that survives a harness reseat needs electronic control replacement.

title: Maytag Dryer F15 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F15 EEPROM data error. Unplug to reset; persistent F15 needs new main electronic control.
meaning: F15 means readable EEPROM returned data outside expected parameters—cycle times, temperature tables, or motor profiles may be invalid.
causes: Interrupted firmware write during a prior service flash
Control board replaced with wrong variant
Long-term heat exposure in a hot garage install
Failed voltage regulator on the board corrupting writes
fixes: Unplug overnight and retry once
Confirm the installed control part number matches the model tech sheet
Check for adequate ventilation around the control mount
Avoid repeated power cycling if F15 returns instantly
when_to_call_technician: Immediate F15 on every plug-in requires the correct replacement control ordered by model and serial.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F2E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag dishwasher F2E2 UI communication fault. Reseat ribbon cable between touch panel and main board.
meaning: F2E2 means the touch user interface lost communication with the main control board. Cycle selection and status feedback stop until the UI harness reconnects.
causes: Ribbon cable loose at the door interface board
Corrosion on pins after steam exposure
Broken trace in the door harness from hinge flexing
Failed UI or main control not acknowledging handshake
fixes: Shut off power at the breaker for safety
Remove the inner door panel and reseat the UI ribbon cable
Inspect pins for green oxidation and clean with electrical contact cleaner
Restore power and run a one-minute cancel drain test
when_to_call_technician: F2E2 with a verified good ribbon cable usually requires UI board replacement on Maytag MDB series units.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F3E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F3E2 pressure switch error. Check the air dome hose from the tub to the control.
meaning: F3E2 indicates the wash tank pressure sensor signal is missing or out of range. Fill volume and overflow protection depend on this airtight hose circuit.
causes: Pressure hose disconnected at the tub air dome
Food sludge blocking the air dome fitting
Hose cracked from heat at the tub connection
Failed pressure sensor on the control board
fixes: Remove power and pull the dishwasher forward for rear access
Trace the clear hose from the tub to the pressure switch
Blow through the hose to confirm it is open
Reinstall without kinks and run a short fill test
when_to_call_technician: Clear hose with recurring F3E2 during fill needs pressure switch or main control service.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F4E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F4E2 wash pump fault. Check for glass in the sump, verify pump connector, listen for hum at wash.
meaning: F4E2 means the main wash circulation pump failed to start or report correct feedback. Dishes stay dirty when wash arms do not pressurize.
causes: Glass shard or seed lodged in the pump impeller
Loose pump connector under the tub
Seized pump bearing from hard water scale
Control relay not sending voltage to the pump
fixes: Cancel the cycle and remove standing water with a towel
Remove the lower spray arm and filter to inspect the sump
Spin the impeller by hand when power is off—it should turn freely
Restart a normal wash and listen for pump hum in the first two minutes
when_to_call_technician: Pump hums without water movement or F4E2 with a free impeller requires circulation pump replacement.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F5E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F5E2 door switch fault. Adjust strike, inspect latch microswitch, replace if door closed but code remains.
meaning: F5E2 means the control never saw the door switch close. Maytag dishwashers block fill and heat until the latch confirms the door is sealed.
causes: Misaligned door strike not pressing the switch
Broken latch hook on the door
Switch lever stuck from detergent buildup
Wire broken in the door harness at the hinge
fixes: Close the door firmly until the latch clicks
Inspect the strike plate on the tub for looseness
Clean the latch receiver with a damp cloth
Run a cycle and listen for the fill valve only after a confirmed latch click
when_to_call_technician: Door switch or latch assembly replacement when the door appears shut but F5E2 repeats.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F6E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F6E2 board communication fault. Reseat main and UI harnesses, check for water on connectors.
meaning: F6E2 is a general communication failure between distributed boards—often the main control and the user interface or dispenser module.
causes: Moisture on a harness connector after a tub leak
Loose 10-pin link between main and UI boards
Power surge damaging UART lines
Substitute control board with wrong firmware
fixes: Disconnect power and dry any moisture near board connectors
Reseat every multi-wire harness on the main control
Inspect for burnt pins near the communication header
Power up and enter diagnostic mode if the model supports it
when_to_call_technician: F6E2 after drying and reseating all connectors typically needs main control or UI replacement as a matched set.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F7E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F7E2 heater error. Test element continuity, inspect hi-limit thermostat, verify water level before heat.
meaning: F7E2 means the dishwasher could not heat water to the target temperature in time. Sanitize and heavy cycles depend on the metal tub heater under the sump.
causes: Open heating element from scale fatigue
Hi-limit thermostat tripped from running dry heat
Loose wire at the heater terminal boot
Insufficient water in tub from a fill fault
fixes: Ensure the dishwasher filled normally before heat step
Remove power and inspect heater terminals for burn marks
Measure element resistance—open circuit means replacement
Reset the hi-limit if the model uses a manual reset thermostat
when_to_call_technician: New element with correct ohms but persistent F7E2 points to relay or control board heat circuit service.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F8E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F8E2 diverter error on wash-zone models. Check diverter motor under filter, clear food debris.
meaning: F8E2 appears on Maytag models with a diverter motor that routes water between upper and lower spray zones. The control cannot confirm the diverter reached the commanded position.
causes: Food debris jamming the diverter disc
Failed diverter motor actuator
Loose connector at the diverter under the filter
Control not driving the diverter during zone switch
fixes: Remove the lower filter and inspect the diverter area
Manually rotate the diverter when power is off to feel for binding
Clear seeds or pasta pieces from the diverter track
Run a normal cycle and listen for diverter movement at zone changes
when_to_call_technician: Free-moving diverter with F8E2 every cycle needs diverter motor W10537869 or equivalent replacement.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F9E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F9E2 dispenser error. Clean wax motor latch, verify dispenser door opens mid-cycle.
meaning: F9E2 means the detergent dispenser did not open or report open when commanded. Main wash chemistry may be missing, leaving greasy dishes.
causes: Detergent caked in the dispenser lid hinge
Failed wax motor on the dispenser assembly
Warped dispenser door from heat cycles
Loose wire to the dispenser actuator
fixes: Scrape old detergent from the dispenser cup and lid
Run hot water at the sink before starting to improve dissolve
Listen for a click opening the dispenser early in wash
Manually flip the dispenser door—it should spring back freely
when_to_call_technician: Dispenser wax motor replacement when the door never pops open during service test.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F1E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F1E2 dishwasher control memory error. Unplug five minutes, replace main board if fault persists.
meaning: F1E2 signals the main control failed an EEPROM integrity check at startup. Cycle logic and timing tables may be unreliable until the board is replaced.
causes: Voltage spike on the dishwasher circuit
Water leak dripping on the control mount
Loose neutral causing brownouts
Age-related memory failure on the board
fixes: Shut off the breaker for five minutes
Inspect under the dishwasher for active leaks onto the control box
Reseat the main harness after drying any moisture
Run a quick rinse cycle as a functional test
when_to_call_technician: Immediate F1E2 on every power-up requires main control board replacement.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F2E1 Error Code
summary: Stuck key on the touch panel
meta_description: Maytag F2E1 stuck touchpad key. Dry the panel, power-cycle, replace UI if a key reads pressed constantly.
meaning: F2E1 means the touch interface reports a key held down. The dishwasher will not respond to new commands until the stuck key state clears.
causes: Moisture under the touch overlay after steam exposure
Detergent film bridging touch pads
Cracked UI glass shorting pads
Failed touch controller on the interface board
fixes: Leave the door open to dry the panel after a hot cycle
Wipe the touch surface with a dry microfiber cloth
Unplug or flip the breaker for two minutes
Avoid pressing multiple keys during restart
when_to_call_technician: Replace the touch UI assembly when F2E1 shows on a dry untouched panel.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F3E1 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F3E1 water temperature sensor fault. Reseat thermistor at the sump, measure resistance at room temp.
meaning: F3E1 means the wash thermistor sent an invalid reading. The control cannot verify water temperature for heat steps and drying efficiency.
causes: Thermistor connector loose at the sump
Scale coating on the sensor bulb
Open thermistor wire in the door-tub harness
Shorted sensor from water intrusion
fixes: Remove power and reseat the thermistor plug at the bottom of the tub
Clean the sensor tip with a soft brush
Compare resistance to the service chart at 77°F
Run a cycle with hot water primed at the kitchen tap
when_to_call_technician: Out-of-spec thermistor readings with good wiring require sensor or harness replacement.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F4E1 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F4E1 turbidity sensor error. Clean the optical sensor in the sump, check wiring to the control.
meaning: F4E1 indicates the turbidity or optical water sensor failed self-check. Auto soil sensing cycles cannot adjust wash time without this input.
causes: Grease film on the optical sensor window
Sensor unplugged after filter service
Cloudy water from detergent leaving false readings
Failed sensor board in the sump assembly
fixes: Remove the lower filter and wipe the clear sensor lens gently
Verify the sensor harness clicks fully into place
Run a cycle with a lightly soiled load after cleaning the lens
Use fresh detergent to avoid excessive clouding
when_to_call_technician: Clean sensor with recurring F4E1 needs turbidity sensor assembly replacement.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F5E1 Error Code
summary: Float switch open—suds or overflow path
meta_description: Maytag F5E1 float switch fault. Check for suds overflow, inspect float under the tub pan.
meaning: F5E1 means the float switch circuit read open when the control expected continuity. Suds overflow or a stuck float can trigger this safety code.
causes: Excess suds lifting the float in the pan
Float stuck in the up position from debris
Open wire to the float switch
Actual water in the base pan from a hose leak
fixes: Use only dishwasher-specific detergent at the marked fill line
Remove the toe kick and check the float moves freely down
Dry any water in the base pan with towels
Run a rinse cycle after clearing suds
when_to_call_technician: Water returns to the pan after drying—find the leak at inlet valve, door gasket, or pump seal before resetting.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F6E1 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F6E1 inlet valve error. Test valve for 120V during fill, clean screens, replace valve if stuck.
meaning: F6E1 means the water inlet valve did not respond correctly when the control commanded fill. The tub may stay dry or fill very slowly.
causes: Clogged inlet screen at the household valve
Inlet valve solenoid open or shorted
Kinked copper supply line under the sink
Control relay not energizing the valve
fixes: Open the supply valve fully and disconnect the line to flush the screen
Listen for valve buzz at fill start—silence means no voltage or dead coil
Verify at least 20 psi water pressure at the dishwasher connection
Run a fill-only diagnostic if available on your model
when_to_call_technician: 120 VAC at the valve with no water flow requires inlet valve W10872255 or model-specific replacement.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F7E1 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F7E1 soil sensor comm error. Clean sensor lens, reseat harness at sump, replace sensor if needed.
meaning: F7E1 is a communication-specific fault for the soil-sensing module—distinct from F4E1 optical failure. Auto cycles may default to maximum time.
causes: Intermittent harness at the turbidity sensor
Sensor module flooded from a cracked sump cover
Software mismatch after partial board replacement
Corrosion on low-voltage sensor pins
fixes: Clean the sensor window and dry the connector area
Reseat the sensor harness under the filter module
Run a normal cycle with medium soil load to test auto sensing
Power-cycle at the breaker for two minutes
when_to_call_technician: F7E1 after sensor and harness replacement attempts needs the turbidity sensor assembly or main control.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 22
meta_description: Maytag fridge code 22 means freezer fan fault. Check ice blocking blade, door switch, fan motor connector.
meaning: Code 22 on Maytag French-door and side-by-side refrigerators means the freezer evaporator fan did not report rotation. Cold air stops circulating from the freezer into the fresh food section.
causes: Ice bridge around the fan shroud after a defrost failure
Door left open allowing heavy frost on the evaporator
Fan motor connector loose behind the freezer back panel
Failed evaporator fan motor bearing
fixes: Unplug and remove freezer shelves to access the back panel
Melt ice blocking the fan blade with a hair dryer on low
Spin the fan by hand when power is off—it should turn freely
Restore power and listen for fan start within 30 seconds of closing the door
when_to_call_technician: Fan motor with no rotation and clear blades needs W11025984 or model-matched evaporator fan replacement.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 23
meta_description: Maytag refrigerator 23 defrost heater fault. Test heater continuity behind freezer panel, check fuse on some models.
meaning: Code 23 indicates the adaptive defrost system detected an open defrost heater circuit. Frost will accumulate on the evaporator until airflow fails.
causes: Open defrost heater element under the evaporator coils
Defrost thermal fuse blown from prolonged heater run
Loose spade connector on the heater harness
Ice choking the defrost drain causing repeated heat overload
fixes: Unplug and remove the freezer rear panel to expose the evaporator
Inspect the heater wire for breaks along the coil loop
Test continuity through the heater and thermal fuse
Clear the drain tube while the panel is off
when_to_call_technician: Open heater or blown defrost fuse requires part replacement and a forced defrost verification.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 24
meta_description: Maytag fridge code 24 defrost thermostat fault. Test bimetal thermostat on evaporator coil.
meaning: Code 24 means the defrost thermostat did not close when the heater energized. The board interprets this as a failed defrost temperature path.
causes: Defrost thermostat stuck open from age
Poor contact at the thermostat clamp on the coil
Thermostat wire broken at the evaporator bend
Control board not sending heat long enough to close the stat
fixes: Locate the round bimetal thermostat clipped to the evaporator
Test for continuity at freezer temperature—should open near 50°F
Reseat the harness at the thermostat
Force a manual defrost through service mode if supported
when_to_call_technician: Replace the defrost thermostat W10384183 family part when it never closes during a forced defrost.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 25
meta_description: Maytag refrigerator 25 long defrost fault. Check stuck damper, failed thermostat, control board defrost algorithm.
meaning: Code 25 appears when a defrost cycle exceeded the maximum allowed time without the evaporator thermistor reporting thaw completion.
causes: Heavy ice load requiring extended manual defrost
Defrost thermistor giving false cold readings
Heater partially open producing slow thaw
Main control defrost timer logic fault
fixes: Manually defrost the freezer with power off and doors open 24 hours
Inspect the thermistor clipped to the evaporator for ice encasement
Verify the heater draws expected amperage during defrost
Clear the drain pan tube before restarting
when_to_call_technician: Code 25 after full manual defrost and good heater amps may need main control or thermistor replacement.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 26
summary: Compressor relay or start device fault
meta_description: Maytag fridge 26 compressor start fault. Listen for click-no-run, test relay and overload on the compressor.
meaning: Code 26 means the compressor did not start when the control energized the start device. Both sections warm when the sealed system is not pumping.
causes: Failed PTC start relay on the compressor
Open compressor overload protector
Locked compressor rotor from liquid slugging
Loose wire on the compressor terminal block
fixes: Unplug and remove the rear lower cover to access the compressor
Inspect the start relay and overload for burn marks
Shake the relay—rattle often indicates failure on PTC types
Plug in and listen for one click then hum without continued running
when_to_call_technician: New relay with no compressor run and hot overload indicates sealed system service.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 27
meta_description: Maytag refrigerator 27 freezer sensor error. Reseat thermistor connector, measure resistance at coil.
meaning: Code 27 means the freezer compartment thermistor sent an out-of-range signal. Defrost timing and compressor run length depend on accurate freezer temperature feedback.
causes: Thermistor connector loose at the evaporator cover
Broken wire in the door hinge harness on dispenser models
Thermistor bulb detached from the evaporator clip
Shorted sensor from water in the connector
fixes: Unplug and reseat the freezer thermistor plug behind the evaporator cover
Inspect wires for pinch at the cabinet liner
Compare resistance to the Maytag service chart at room temperature
Allow 24 hours stable temps after repair before judging performance
when_to_call_technician: Out-of-spec thermistor with intact wiring requires sensor W10316760 or equivalent.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 28
meta_description: Maytag fridge code 28 refrigerator sensor error. Check thermistor in the fresh food airflow duct.
meaning: Code 28 indicates the refrigerator section thermistor failed. The control may over-cool or under-cool the fresh food area without valid readings.
causes: Thermistor pulled out of the airflow duct clip
Corrosion on pins at the main board connector
Open sensor wire behind the crisper assembly
Control board input circuit damaged
fixes: Locate the thermistor in the upper fresh food duct near the light
Press it fully into the mounting clip
Reseat the harness at the control board in the ceiling
Monitor fresh food temps for 12 hours after repair
when_to_call_technician: Correct resistance at the sensor plug but persistent code 28 points to main control replacement.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 29
summary: Ice maker optics or position fault
meta_description: Maytag refrigerator 29 ice maker error. Align emitter and receiver boards, check ice jam in ejector.
meaning: Code 29 appears on models with optical ice level sensing. The control cannot confirm ice maker position or bin level.
causes: Ice mound blocking the emitter and receiver LEDs
Misaligned optics boards after shelf removal
Ice maker module stalled mid-rotation
Loose connector at the ice maker head
fixes: Level the ice in the bin below the sensor path
Remove the ice maker cover and verify both optics boards face each other
Manually rotate the ejector if a cube is jammed—power off first
Press the feeler arm down to reset the harvest cycle
when_to_call_technician: Optics aligned with no ice obstruction but code 29 every harvest needs ice maker module W10884390 series service.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 30
meta_description: Maytag fridge 30 damper fault. Listen for damper motor in fresh food ceiling, clear ice blocking door.
meaning: Code 30 means the motorized damper between freezer and refrigerator did not open. The fresh food section warms while the freezer may still cool.
causes: Ice buildup freezing the damper door shut
Failed damper motor actuator
Food package blocking damper grille
Control not driving the damper motor
fixes: Remove items blocking the damper grille in the upper refrigerator
Listen for damper movement when the door switch is released
Defrost any visible ice at the damper housing with power off
Verify freezer fan runs—no fan airflow mimics damper failure
when_to_call_technician: Damper motor with no movement after defrost requires W10814173 or model-specific damper assembly.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 31
meta_description: Maytag refrigerator 31 ice chute door error. Check ice buildup on dispenser door, test motor in door.
meaning: Code 31 means the in-door ice chute flap did not open or close on command. Cubes may jam or warm air enters the freezer through the chute.
causes: Ice frozen around the chute door in the dispenser
Failed chute door motor or linkage
Broken door return spring
Dispenser board not sending voltage to the motor
fixes: Clear visible ice from the dispenser chute with warm cloth
Activate crushed and cubed ice to exercise the door
Inspect the chute for a cube wedged against the flap
Power-cycle the refrigerator for two minutes
when_to_call_technician: Chute door motor replacement when the flap never moves after clearing ice.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 32
summary: Dispenser water valve or switch fault
meta_description: Maytag fridge 32 water dispenser error. Test water valve at bottom grille, replace filter, check switch.
meaning: Code 32 indicates the dispenser water system failed—valve, switch, or heater circuit on chilled-water models.
causes: Clogged water filter restricting flow
Frozen water line in the door on chilled dispensers
Dispenser microswitch stuck
Dual water valve not opening for the door circuit
fixes: Replace the water filter if overdue per Maytag schedule
Dispense two gallons to purge air after filter change
Test household water supply at the saddle valve
Hold the paddle and listen for valve buzz at the bottom front
when_to_call_technician: Valve buzz with no water after new filter may need door tube thawing or valve W10865826 replacement.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 33
summary: Water filter status or flow fault
meta_description: Maytag refrigerator 33 filter error. Install correct EveryDrop filter, reset filter indicator, check for blockage.
meaning: Code 33 relates to the water filter circuit—either excessive restriction, missing filter, or RFID mismatch on models with filter sensing.
causes: Overdue filter loaded with sediment
Non-OEM filter not seating the RFID chip
Air lock after filter change
Filter head O-rings twisted during install
fixes: Install Maytag EveryDrop EDR1RXD1 or model-listed filter
Run five gallons through the dispenser to clear air
Press and hold the filter reset button per your owner's guide
Inspect for leaks at the filter housing after install
when_to_call_technician: Slow flow with a new OEM filter may indicate a clogged valve or need for housing service.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 34
meta_description: Maytag fridge 34 sealed system error. Both sections warm, compressor runs long—needs refrigeration technician.
meaning: Code 34 means the control detected poor cooling performance consistent with a sealed system issue—low refrigerant, restriction, or weak compressor pumping.
causes: Partial refrigerant loss from a micro-leak
Cap tube restriction at the evaporator inlet
Weak compressor valves after years of run time
Condenser fan not removing heat from the loop
fixes: Verify the condenser fan runs when the compressor is on
Clean the condenser coils under or behind the unit
Confirm both sections are warm despite a running compressor
Document temps before calling for sealed system diagnosis
when_to_call_technician: Code 34 with a running compressor and clean coils requires EPA-certified sealed system repair.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 35
meta_description: Maytag refrigerator 35 UI error. Reseat display harness at door top, check for condensation on connector.
meaning: Code 35 means the dispenser or console UI lost communication with the main control. Temperature displays and ice/water requests may freeze.
causes: Loose ribbon cable at the dispenser UI
Condensation on connectors in the door after humid weather
Power surge damaging the UI board
Door harness wire broken at the hinge
fixes: Unplug and remove the dispenser facade to reseat ribbons
Dry any moisture on connectors with a cloth
Inspect the hinge area for chafed wires
Restore power and test display response
when_to_call_technician: Good harness continuity with dead display segments needs UI board W11174836 or equivalent.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 36
summary: Main control to UI serial link fault
meta_description: Maytag fridge 36 communication error between boards. Reseat all door and ceiling harnesses.
meaning: Code 36 is a broader communication fault between the main machine control and distributed boards—UI, ice maker, or damper modules.
causes: Corroded CAN or serial connector at the main board
Substitute control with incompatible software
Intermittent neutral causing board resets
Failed main control UART driver
fixes: Unplug 24 hours if recent lightning—then reseat every harness
Verify all connectors at the main control in the ceiling
Check for error codes stored in combination with 35
Record model and serial before ordering boards
when_to_call_technician: Code 36 after full harness service often requires main control and matched UI replacement.

title: Maytag Oven F03 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag oven F03 RTD sensor open. Check probe connector behind range, measure resistance at room temp.
meaning: F03 on Maytag wall ovens and ranges means the oven temperature sensor (RTD) circuit is open. The board cannot regulate bake or broil without a valid probe signal.
causes: Sensor plug loose behind the rear panel
Broken sensor wire inside the oven cavity routing
Failed RTD probe internally open
Connector corrosion from steam at the broil element
fixes: Shut off power at the breaker
Remove the rear lower panel and reseat the sensor harness
Measure probe resistance—about 1080 ohms at room temperature on many models
Replace the probe if resistance reads infinite
when_to_call_technician: New probe with correct ohms but persistent F03 indicates main clock or ERC wiring service.

title: Maytag Oven F04 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F04 shorted oven sensor. Inspect probe for bare wire touching cavity, replace RTD if shorted.
meaning: F04 means the oven temperature sensor reads a short circuit. The control disables heat to prevent uncontrolled temperature rise.
causes: Sensor wire pinched against the oven liner
Moisture shorting the connector after steam cleaning
Failed probe with internal short
Metal braid touching terminal screws
fixes: Disconnect power and inspect the sensor wire route through the cavity
Dry the connector thoroughly if wet
Measure resistance—near zero ohms indicates a shorted probe
Install a new W10181986-style probe matched to your model
when_to_call_technician: F04 with a disconnected probe means a harness short—trace wiring to the ERC.

title: Maytag Oven F05 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F05 door lock error on self-clean models. Check latch motor, wiring, and door alignment.
meaning: F05 appears on self-cleaning Maytag ovens when the door latch cannot lock or unlock on command. Clean and convection cycles may be blocked until the latch reports correct position.
causes: Food debris on the latch hook preventing engagement
Failed latch motor actuator
Warped door hinge misaligning the strike
Broken wire in the door switch harness
fixes: Cancel any active clean cycle and allow the oven to cool
Inspect the latch slot on the door frame for grease buildup
Listen for latch motor movement when selecting self-clean
Verify the door closes flush against the gasket
when_to_call_technician: Latch motor with no movement after cleaning needs W10307058 or model-specific latch assembly.

title: Maytag Oven F06 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F06 door switch error. Test latch switches, verify lock engages before clean cycle.
meaning: F06 means the door lock switch circuit failed verification. The ERC detected an unexpected open or short in the lock position feedback.
causes: Latch switch stuck from carbon after clean cycles
Switch harness unplugged during prior service
Failed dual-switch latch assembly
ERC not reading switch bounce correctly
fixes: Power off and manually move the latch to feel for binding
Inspect the two microswitches on the latch motor
Reseat the latch harness at the ERC
Run a short bake at 350°F before attempting self-clean again
when_to_call_technician: Replace the door latch assembly when switches test bad or latch motor stalls.

title: Maytag Oven F07 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F07 lock motor fault. No latch movement on self-clean—replace door lock motor assembly.
meaning: F07 specifically targets the lock motor output circuit. The ERC commanded latch movement but did not detect motor response.
causes: Burned lock motor windings
Relay on ERC not sending voltage to motor
Mechanical jam in latch linkage
Broken motor gear inside the latch
fixes: Disconnect power before inspecting the latch motor
Check for 120 VAC at the motor during lock command in service mode
Lubricate only per service manual—excess grease attracts dust
Replace latch assembly if motor is open or seized
when_to_call_technician: Voltage at motor with no shaft rotation confirms latch motor replacement.

title: Maytag Oven F08 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F08 cooling fan error. Fan should run during bake on many models—check harness and fan motor.
meaning: F08 means the oven cooling fan did not start when required. Electronic controls and door components depend on airflow during heat operation.
causes: Fan blade obstructed by wiring tie
Open fan motor winding
Loose connector at the rear cooling fan
ERC fan relay failed
fixes: Shut off power and spin the cooling fan blade by hand
Remove the rear panel and reseat the fan harness
Listen for fan start within a minute of turning oven on
Clear any foil or storage against rear vents
when_to_call_technician: Fan motor that won't run with good voltage needs W10822296 or equivalent cooling fan.

title: Maytag Oven F09 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F09 over-temperature fault. Shut off breaker, test sensor and ERC—do not use until cleared.
meaning: F09 is a safety over-temperature fault. The ERC detected oven temperature above the safe limit—often from a welded relay or failed sensor reading low.
causes: Bake relay stuck closed on the ERC
Sensor detached giving false cold reading
Runaway clean cycle with latch fault
External heat source too close to the range
fixes: Turn off the breaker immediately and leave the door closed until cool
Inspect the sensor mounting in the upper oven cavity
Do not restart until F09 cause is identified
Test bake element for continuity to ground when power is off
when_to_call_technician: F09 after cooldown with a good sensor strongly suggests ERC relay failure—replace the control board.

title: Maytag Oven F10 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F10 temperature runaway. Power off, test oven sensor and ERC relays before reuse.
meaning: F10 indicates the oven heated without proportional control—similar to F09 on many Maytag platforms. The ERC shuts down all heat outputs.
causes: Shorted bake element leaking to ground
ERC bake relay welded shut
Sensor reading far below actual cavity temp
Broil element on continuously from relay fault
fixes: Disconnect power at the breaker for 30 minutes
Measure bake element resistance and ground leakage
Verify the sensor reads rising resistance as the cavity cools
Replace sensor first if readings are erratic
when_to_call_technician: Good sensor and element with immediate F10 on preheat needs ERC replacement.

title: Maytag Oven F11 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F11 shorted key fault on oven UI. Dry the panel, replace touchpad if short persists.
meaning: F11 means the oven user interface detected a short on the keypad matrix. Buttons may be unresponsive or trigger randomly.
causes: Liquid spilled on the control panel
Steam from cooktop shorting the membrane
Cracked overlay bridging keys
Failed ERC keypad input driver
fixes: Power off and dry the control area thoroughly
Avoid cleaning with excessive water on the touch panel
Unplug ribbon harness at ERC and inspect for corrosion
Replace touchpad if F11 shows with harness disconnected from UI
when_to_call_technician: Touchpad or ERC replacement depending on whether short clears with UI unplugged.

title: Maytag Oven F12 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F12 stuck oven key. Clean panel, power-cycle—replace UI if a key reads pressed constantly.
meaning: F12 is a stuck-key fault distinct from F11 short. One button appears continuously pressed, blocking other inputs.
causes: Grease under the Start or Cancel pad
Child leaning on the panel during cook
Membrane collapse from age
ERC interpreting noise as a held key
fixes: Clean around all buttons with a damp cloth then dry
Press each key several times to free a stuck pad
Power-cycle at the breaker for two minutes
Avoid using foil on racks touching the door sensor
when_to_call_technician: Replace the oven touchpad when F12 appears with a clean dry panel untouched.

title: Maytag Oven F13 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F13 EEPROM error on oven control. Unplug five minutes, replace ERC if code returns every start.
meaning: F13 means the electronic range control failed a memory checksum at power-up. Stored calibration and timing tables may be corrupt.
causes: Power surge on the range circuit
Moisture on ERC from steam or boil-over
Age-related EEPROM failure
Loose logic supply connector
fixes: Shut off the breaker for five minutes
Inspect ERC area for steam damage behind the console
Reseat all harness plugs into the ERC
Run a short 350°F bake test after reset
when_to_call_technician: Immediate F13 on every power-up requires ERC board replacement matched to model suffix.

title: Maytag Oven F14 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F14 control memory data fault. Treat like F13—inspect harness, replace electronic range control.
meaning: F14 is a secondary EEPROM data fault on the same ERC platform. Cooking functions may be unpredictable until the board is replaced.
causes: Brownout during self-clean
Incorrect board substituted in prior repair
Logic ground loose at ERC mount
Manufacturing defect in memory chip batch
fixes: Verify the installed ERC part number against the tech sheet
Tighten ERC mounting screws for good ground
Avoid running self-clean until fault clears
Document serial number for parts ordering
when_to_call_technician: Persistent F14 after one reset requires a new ERC from Maytag parts.

title: Maytag Oven F15 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F15 oven control comm error. Reseat UI ribbon to ERC, replace board if fault persists.
meaning: F15 indicates internal communication failure within the ERC or between ERC and the touch UI. Display may freeze or show garbage characters.
causes: Loose ribbon cable between touchpad and ERC
Failed ERC microcontroller
Static discharge damaging comm lines
UI and ERC revision mismatch after repair
fixes: Power off and reseat the UI ribbon at both ends
Inspect for burnt pins on the ERC header
Match replacement boards to the exact model revision
Test bake operation after reseat
when_to_call_technician: F15 with verified harness needs ERC or touchpad replacement as a matched set.

title: Maytag Oven F16 Error Code
summary: Communication error between ERC and UI
meta_description: Maytag F16 UI-to-ERC link fault. Check ribbon cable, replace touch panel or control board.
meaning: F16 specifically flags lost serial communication between the display UI and the ERC—common after replacing only one of the two assemblies.
causes: Partial board replacement with incompatible firmware
Cut ribbon cable during console removal
Corrosion on low-voltage comm pins
Failed ERC UART output driver
fixes: Compare part numbers on UI and ERC before mixing boards
Replace the ribbon if creased or torn
Run a keypad test through service mode if available
Power-cycle after any harness work
when_to_call_technician: Order UI and ERC together using model and serial when F16 follows a single-board swap.

title: Maytag Oven F17 Error Code
summary: Probe or meat thermometer circuit fault
meta_description: Maytag F17 temperature probe jack error. Check built-in probe connection, jack wiring to ERC.
meaning: F17 appears on models with a temperature probe jack. The ERC detected an open or short in the external meat probe circuit during convection roast or probe bake.
causes: Probe not fully seated in the jack
Damaged probe cable from kinking
Jack wiring loose at the ERC
Probe tip damaged from high heat exposure
fixes: Insert the probe firmly until it clicks
Test with a known-good Maytag probe if available
Inspect the jack behind the console for loose wires
Cancel probe mode and use timed bake as a workaround
when_to_call_technician: Good probe with F17 at the jack needs receptacle or ERC input circuit repair.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF0 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire EF0 means aqua control activated. Check for leaks in base pan, reduce HE detergent, rebalance load.
meaning: EF0 on Frigidaire front-load washers means the aqua control sensor in the base pan detected moisture, or excessive foam mimicked a leak. The door may lock and the drain pump can run until the condition clears.
causes: Water in the base pan from a door seal or hose leak
Too much non-HE detergent creating foam overflow
Unbalanced load shaking water past the door gasket
Dispenser drawer leaking during fill
fixes: Unplug and tilt carefully to check for water in the base pan—dry with towels
Switch to HE detergent at the lowest recommended dose
Inspect the gray door boot for tears or debris in the fold
Redistribute laundry and run a Spin+Drain cycle
when_to_call_technician: Dry pan with recurring EF0 after seal inspection points to inlet valve or tub hose service.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF1 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire EF1 clogged drain fault. Clean pump filter behind lower panel, check drain hose for kinks.
meaning: EF1 means water could not drain within the expected time—the drain filter is blocked or the pump cannot move water. Frigidaire Electrolux platforms stop the cycle to prevent standing water.
causes: Pump filter packed with coins, hair, and lint
Drain hose kinked behind the washer
Foreign object jamming the pump impeller
Household standpipe partially clogged
fixes: Open the lower front access panel and place a shallow pan under the filter
Slowly unscrew the pump filter and rinse debris
Straighten the drain hose and confirm standpipe height per install guide
Run a Drain/Spin cycle and listen for steady pump operation
when_to_call_technician: EF1 with an empty filter and free hose usually means drain pump motor replacement.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF2 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire EF2 too much soap. Use HE detergent sparingly, run rinse cycle to clear suds.
meaning: EF2 is an over-sudsing fault detected during drain phases. Foam can trip aqua control and prevent the drum from reaching proper spin speed.
causes: Regular detergent instead of HE formula
Double-dosing pods or liquid above the fill line
Soft water requiring even less detergent
Residual suds from prior overdosed cycle
fixes: Reduce detergent to one tablespoon HE for normal loads
Run an empty hot cycle with no detergent to flush suds
Add one cup white vinegar in an empty rinse cycle
Avoid combining pods with liquid softener in the drum
when_to_call_technician: EF2 on bare minimum detergent with soft water may need a pressure sensor check—uncommon.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF3 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire EF3 aqua control lockout. Dry base pan, fix leak source, reset by completing drain.
meaning: EF3 means the aqua control system latched into safety mode—similar to EF0 but often after repeated foam or leak events. The machine may run the drain pump continuously.
causes: Styrofoam float in base pan stuck in up position
Slow leak from tub-to-pump hose clamp
Overfill from inlet valve stuck open
Foam migration into the pan tripping the float
fixes: Disconnect power and water supply
Remove the toe panel and dry the pan completely
Verify the float drops freely when pan is dry
Inspect tub seal and dispenser for active leaks before restart
when_to_call_technician: Water returns to the pan within hours—trace leak at door boot, inlet valve, or sump hose.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF4 Error Code
summary: Low incoming water pressure or flow meter fault
meta_description: Frigidaire EF4 fill pressure fault. Open valves fully, clean inlet screens, verify 20+ PSI supply.
meaning: EF4 appears when fill pressure is too low or the flow meter sees no pulse during fill. The washer cannot confirm adequate water entry.
causes: Partially closed supply valves
Clogged inlet screens at the wall or washer
Flow meter turbine stuck from sediment
Low municipal pressure during peak use
fixes: Open hot and cold valves fully behind the machine
Disconnect hoses and rinse screens into a bucket
Run the kitchen tap at full flow to confirm household pressure
Start a cold fill and watch through the door for incoming water
when_to_call_technician: Strong flow at disconnected hoses with EF4 during fill needs flow meter or inlet valve service.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF5 Error Code
summary: Hot water inlet NTC temperature fault
meta_description: Frigidaire EF5 hot valve thermistor error. Test NTC at mixing valve, replace valve assembly if open.
meaning: EF5 means the hot water inlet thermistor read out of range. Temperature-controlled fills on Electrolux-built Frigidaire washers cannot proceed without valid hot-side feedback.
causes: Thermistor disconnected at the mixing valve
Scale buildup insulating the sensor bulb
Open thermistor element
Control not reading sensor after valve replacement
fixes: Unplug and access the inlet valve on the rear panel
Reseat the two-wire thermistor connector
Measure resistance against the Electrolux chart at room temp
Run warm fill after verifying hot supply at the tap
when_to_call_technician: Replace the inlet valve assembly when the thermistor reads open at 77°F.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF6 Error Code
summary: Motor triac or drive circuit fault
meta_description: Frigidaire EF6 motor control error. Unplug 15 minutes, reduce load—MCU or motor service if repeats.
meaning: EF6 indicates the motor drive triac or inverter stage faulted. The drum may not turn during wash or spin even though the door is locked.
causes: Severely overloaded drum binding the motor
Motor harness loose at the inverter
Failed motor windings shorting the triac
Inverter board overheated from poor ventilation
fixes: Unplug for 15 minutes to cool the inverter
Remove heavy items and test with a small load
Spin the drum by hand when empty—it should move freely
Ensure rear clearance for inverter airflow per install guide
when_to_call_technician: EF6 on a light load after reset needs motor or inverter board diagnosis.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF7 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire EF7 door lock error. Remove fabric from gasket, listen for lock click, replace latch if needed.
meaning: EF7 means the door lock circuit failed—often triggered by foam, leaks, or a broken latch motor. The washer will not fill or spin until lock feedback is valid.
causes: Clothing trapped preventing door closure
Broken door hook on the door assembly
Latch motor burned out from repeated foam events
Wiring broken at the door hinge harness
fixes: Pull fabric from the door boot fold and press door until one click
Power-cycle two minutes and retry
Listen for buzz without click at lock attempt
Inspect door hook alignment with the latch receiver
when_to_call_technician: Door lock assembly 131763202 or model-matched part when hook and gasket are good but EF7 persists.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF8 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire EF8 pressure fault. Check air dome hose from tub to control for kinks and leaks.
meaning: EF8 means the pressure switch or transducer did not report a valid water level during fill or drain.
causes: Pressure hose disconnected at the tub dome
Hose pinched behind the counterweight
Dome port clogged with detergent crust
Failed pressure sensor on the main board
fixes: Remove power and trace the clear hose to the control
Blow through the hose to verify openness
Clean the rubber grommet at the tub connection
Reinstall without sharp bends
when_to_call_technician: Intact hose with EF8 on every fill points to main control pressure input failure.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF9 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire EF9 motor speed sensor fault. Check motor connector, test after light load reset.
meaning: EF9 means the tachometer on the motor did not send speed feedback for several seconds. Spin and wash profiles stop without RPM confirmation.
causes: Motor connector loose at the rear panel
Broken tacho wire in the motor harness
Failed tach coil on the motor
Control board motor feedback circuit fault
fixes: Unplug and reseat the motor harness
Run a small load after 15-minute power-off cool down
Listen for brief motor hum then EF9 on spin
Avoid washing single heavy rugs alone
when_to_call_technician: EF9 on light loads with seated harness needs motor or control board service.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF10 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire EF10 pressure switch error. Inspect hose, air trap, and switch clicking during fill test.
meaning: EF10 is a secondary pressure fault—the switch state did not change when water entered or left the tub.
causes: Air leak at pressure hose clamps
Switch diaphragm stuck from age
Over-suds holding false water level
Board commanding fill while pressure reads full
fixes: Run an empty rinse to clear suds
Remove and reinstall the pressure hose with airtight fit
Listen for switch clicks during a monitored fill
Compare behavior on cold vs hot fill cycles
when_to_call_technician: Good hose with no switch transitions during fill needs pressure switch or board replacement.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF11 Error Code
summary: Heating circuit or NTC wash temperature fault
meta_description: Frigidaire EF11 heater error. Verify warm water at tap, test wash NTC and heating element.
meaning: EF11 means the washer could not heat water to the selected temperature—common on models with internal heaters or strict temperature monitoring.
causes: Household water heater set too low
Open heating element circuit
Wash NTC thermistor detached
Tripped thermal cutout on heater can
fixes: Run the hot tap 30 seconds before starting a warm cycle
Select 40°C or higher and feel door glass mid-cycle
Inspect heater terminals behind the rear panel when powered off
Reset thermal fuse if the model uses a one-time cutout
when_to_call_technician: No warmth on sanitize with verified hot fill needs element or NTC replacement.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF12 Error Code
summary: Communication error between UI and control
meta_description: Frigidaire EF12 board comm fault. Reseat console ribbon cable, inspect for corrosion.
meaning: EF12 means the display board and main control lost serial communication. Buttons and cycle progress may freeze.
causes: Ribbon cable loose at the console
Moisture on UI connector after leak
Power surge damaging comm chip
Mismatched UI after partial repair
fixes: Unplug and remove the top console to reseat ribbons
Dry connectors if any moisture is present
Power-cycle five minutes
Test with a basic Cotton cycle
when_to_call_technician: EF12 after ribbon reseat needs UI board or main control as a matched pair.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF13 Error Code
summary: Control EEPROM or memory verification failed
meta_description: Frigidaire EF13 memory fault. Unplug five minutes, replace main board if code returns every start.
meaning: EF13 indicates corrupted non-volatile memory on the main control. Cycle tables and calibration may be invalid.
causes: Voltage spike on laundry circuit
Incomplete power loss during update
Water drip on control from dispenser leak
Age-related EEPROM failure
fixes: Shut off breaker five minutes
Inspect control mount for water stains
Reseat all harnesses into the main board
Avoid extension cords that cause voltage sag
when_to_call_technician: Immediate EF13 on power-up requires main control replacement.

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF14 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire EF14 balance fault. Redistribute wet laundry, level machine, inspect shocks and springs.
meaning: EF14 means repeated unbalance detection—the drum could not reach spin speed after multiple redistribution attempts.
causes: Single heavy item like a comforter alone
Worn shock absorbers or broken suspension spring
Washer not level on the floor
Transit bolts left installed on a new delivery
fixes: Pause and spread wet items evenly around the drum
Add towels when washing one heavy piece
Level the feet with a bubble level and lock nuts
Remove shipping bolts if the washer is newly installed
when_to_call_technician: EF14 on balanced full loads after leveling needs suspension shock replacement.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E01 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E01 door switch fault. Close door firmly, replace switch if drum won't run.
meaning: E01 means the door switch never reported closed. Frigidaire dryers block the motor until the plunger is fully depressed.
causes: Worn door catch
Broken switch lever
Door hinge sag
Failed door switch
fixes: Close door until drum light goes out
Inspect plunger through front gap
Replace switch if no run with door shut
Tighten hinge screws
when_to_call_technician: Door switch is a common owner-replaceable part on FREQ models.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E02 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E02 stuck keypad. Dry panel, power-cycle, replace UI if key reads pressed.
meaning: E02 indicates a continuously activated keypad. Cycle selection is blocked.
causes: Moisture on membrane
Detergent residue on UI
Cracked overlay
Failed UI board
fixes: Wipe console dry
Unplug two minutes
Press each key once after restart
Replace UI if untouched panel shows E02
when_to_call_technician: UI replacement when cleaning does not clear E02.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E03 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E03 thermistor fault. Reseat sensor at blower, measure resistance.
meaning: E03 means the exhaust temperature sensor circuit is open. Heat modulation stops without valid exhaust feedback.
causes: Loose thermistor plug
Broken wire at blower
Open sensor element
Lint shorting connector intermittently
fixes: Unplug and reseat thermistor at blower housing
Vacuum lint from connector
Measure ohms at room temp per chart
Run timed dry on low heat
when_to_call_technician: Open thermistor with good wiring needs sensor replacement.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E04 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E04 moisture bar fault. Clean sensor strips inside drum, check harness.
meaning: E04 means auto-dry moisture bars read open. Sensor dry cycles may end too early or not start.
causes: Sensor harness unplugged
Fabric softener coating bars
Corroded terminals
Open sensor wire
fixes: Clean bars with rubbing alcohol
Verify harness behind bulkhead
Run Sensor Dry on damp load
Inspect door harness for breaks
when_to_call_technician: Persistent E04 with clean bars needs harness or sensor service.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E05 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E05 thermal fuse open. Clean vent, test fuse on blower housing, replace if blown.
meaning: E05 indicates the exhaust thermal fuse opened from overheating—usually restricted vent airflow.
causes: Crushed vent hose
Lint-choked wall duct
Blocked exterior cap
Cycling thermostat failure causing high heat
fixes: Clean lint screen and full vent run
Test thermal fuse for continuity
Replace fuse only after restoring airflow
Run empty timed cycle and feel exterior vent
when_to_call_technician: Fuse blows again within one cycle—deep vent cleaning or thermostat service needed.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E06 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E06 UI comm fault. Reseat console harness to main control.
meaning: E06 means the user interface and main control lost communication.
causes: Loose UI ribbon
Surge damage
Corroded connector
Mismatched board pair
fixes: Unplug and reseat UI harness
Inspect ribbon for creases
Power-cycle five minutes
Match board revisions when replacing
when_to_call_technician: E06 after reseat typically needs UI board first.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E07 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E07 shorted moisture sensor. Dry bars and harness, replace strip if needed.
meaning: E07 is a shorted moisture sensor circuit—opposite of E04.
causes: Water on sensor harness
Metal debris bridging bars
Pinched wire
Failed sensor strip
fixes: Dry interior bars and harness
Disconnect sensor and test for short
Run timed dry until repaired
Replace moisture sensor if short persists
when_to_call_technician: Sensor replacement when short remains with harness unplugged.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E08 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E08 static fault. Ground yourself before touching controls in dry winter air.
meaning: E08 is a static-sensitive keypad fault in low-humidity conditions.
causes: Dry winter air
Synthetic loads building static
Ungrounded outlet
Sensitive UI design
fixes: Touch grounded metal before using panel
Unplug one minute and retry
Add humidity to laundry room
Verify three-prong grounded outlet
when_to_call_technician: E08 every start with verified ground may need UI replacement.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E09 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E09 incompatible UI and control software. Install matched board pair.
meaning: E09 appears when UI and main control firmware revisions do not match after partial replacement.
causes: Single board replaced with wrong rev
Factory flash error
Aftermarket control
Incomplete pairing after repair
fixes: Record model and serial from rating plate
Order matched UI and control set
Avoid mixing boards from different revisions
Power-cycle after installing both boards
when_to_call_technician: Replace UI and control together when E09 follows one-board swap.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E10 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E10 power fault. Test 120V at terminal block, tighten cord connections.
meaning: E10 means line voltage fell outside the safe operating window.
causes: Loose terminal block screws
Shared circuit voltage sag
Weak breaker contact
Long undersized extension cord
fixes: Test outlet voltage under load
Tighten cord strain relief and terminals
Use dedicated 30A dryer circuit
Reset breaker fully off then on
when_to_call_technician: Low voltage at terminal with good outlet indicates house wiring issue.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E11 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E11 shorted key. Dry console thoroughly after spills.
meaning: E11 means the UI detected a hard short on the keypad matrix.
causes: Water spill on console
Shorted membrane
Liquid softener on panel
Failed UI board
fixes: Power off and dry panel hours
Inspect for moisture under overlay
Replace UI if dry panel still shows E11
Keep liquids away from controls
when_to_call_technician: UI replacement is the typical fix for persistent E11.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E12 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E12 bad relay. Heat or motor may not start—control board replacement likely.
meaning: E12 indicates a drive relay on the main control failed self-test.
causes: Welded heat relay
Burnt driver transistor
Moisture on relay bank
Motor overload stressing relay
fixes: Unplug five minutes and test one cycle
Inspect board for burnt relay areas
Note model before ordering control
Do not bypass safety components
when_to_call_technician: E12 requires main electronic control replacement.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E13 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E13 memory fault. Power-cycle; replace control if immediate on every start.
meaning: E13 means control memory failed checksum at power-up.
causes: Brownout
Surge
Incomplete power loss
EEPROM age failure
fixes: Unplug ten minutes
Reseat main harness
Avoid voltage dips from extension cords
Run short air-fluff test
when_to_call_technician: Recurring E13 needs new main control.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E14 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E14 alternate memory fault. Inspect harness, replace board.
meaning: E14 is a second checksum fault path on the control platform.
causes: Lightning surge
Water drip on control
Loose logic supply
Wrong control variant installed
fixes: Verify correct control part number
Dry any moisture near board
Reseat all connectors
Document serial for parts
when_to_call_technician: E14 after harness service needs control replacement.

title: Frigidaire Dryer E15 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire E15 data error. Replace main control if fault returns instantly.
meaning: E15 means stored cycle data read back invalid from EEPROM.
causes: Interrupted memory write
Heat exposure in garage install
Voltage regulator failure
Wrong board firmware
fixes: Unplug overnight once
Confirm board matches tech sheet
Ensure venting around control
Avoid rapid power cycling
when_to_call_technician: Instant E15 on plug-in needs correct replacement control.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher i30 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire i30 flood float triggered. Dry base pan, fix leak, pump runs until float drops.
meaning: i30 means water collected in the bottom pan and lifted the styrofoam float. The drain pump may run continuously as an anti-flood measure.
causes: Loose drain hose clamp at sink
Door gasket tear
Over-suds pushing water past seal
Cracked inlet valve body
fixes: Shut off power and water
Remove toe kick and dry the pan with towels
Confirm float drops freely when dry
Inspect door seal and under-sink hose clamps
when_to_call_technician: Water returns to pan after drying—trace leak before replacing control.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher i40 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire i40 drain fault. Clean glass trap, filter, and drain hose—check air gap.
meaning: i40 indicates water could not drain within the timeout—same family as i20 with a secondary drain fault flag.
causes: Glass trap and filter clogged
Kinked drain hose
Blocked air gap at sink
Garbage disposal knockout plug still in place
fixes: Remove and rinse the glass trap and fine filter
Verify drain hose has high loop or air gap
Run disposal to clear shared drain
Start a cancel-drain and listen for pump hum
when_to_call_technician: i40 with clear path and humming pump needs drain pump motor.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher i50 Error Code
summary: Drain pump or wash motor failure
meta_description: Frigidaire i50 motor fault. Check sump for glass, test pump connector, verify impeller spins.
meaning: i50 means the drain or circulation motor failed to start or report feedback.
causes: Glass shard in pump impeller
Seized pump bearing
Loose motor connector
Control relay not energizing motor
fixes: Cancel cycle and towel out standing water
Remove lower spray arm and clean sump
Spin impeller by hand when power is off
Restart and listen for motor at wash and drain steps
when_to_call_technician: Motor hums without movement needs pump or circulation motor replacement.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher i60 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire i60 heater error. Test element ohms, check hi-limit thermostat at sump.
meaning: i60 means the dishwasher could not heat water to the required temperature for wash or dry.
causes: Open heating element
Hi-limit thermostat tripped
Loose wire at heater boot
Insufficient water from fill fault
fixes: Confirm tub filled before heat step
Remove power and inspect heater terminals
Measure element resistance
Run hot water at sink before starting
when_to_call_technician: New element with correct ohms but i60 needs relay or board heat circuit.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher i70 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire i70 thermistor error. Reseat sensor at sump, measure resistance at room temp.
meaning: i70 indicates the wash thermistor sent invalid readings.
causes: Thermistor unplugged
Scale on sensor bulb
Open sensor wire
Shorted connector from water
fixes: Reseat thermistor plug under the tub
Clean sensor tip gently
Compare ohms to service chart at 77°F
Prime hot water at tap before cycle
when_to_call_technician: Out-of-range thermistor with good wiring needs sensor replacement.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher i80 Error Code
summary: Vent fan or drying system fault
meta_description: Frigidaire i80 vent error. Check vent fan at top of door, clear blockage, test fan motor.
meaning: i80 relates to the vented drying system—fan not running or vent stuck open/closed.
causes: Vent fan motor failed
Vent flap stuck closed
Wiring loose at vent assembly
Control not driving fan during dry
fixes: Inspect vent grille at top of door for obstruction
Listen for fan during dry phase
Reseat vent harness behind inner door panel
Run a heated dry cycle as test
when_to_call_technician: No fan movement with voltage at vent motor needs vent assembly service.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher i90 Error Code
summary: Water inlet valve or fill timeout
meta_description: Frigidaire i90 fill fault. Open supply, clean inlet screen, test valve during fill.
meaning: i90 means inadequate water entered the tub within the fill window.
causes: Closed supply valve
Clogged inlet screen
Kinked copper line
Inlet valve solenoid failed
fixes: Open valve fully under sink
Disconnect line and flush screen
Listen for valve buzz at fill start
Verify 20+ PSI at connection
when_to_call_technician: 120V at valve with no flow needs inlet valve 154637401 or equivalent.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher iA0 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire iA0 control fault. Unplug five minutes, replace main board if code persists.
meaning: iA0 indicates sudden main control board failure—iC0 on some displays is the same class of fault.
causes: Power surge
Water on control from leak
Failed MCU processor
Loose neutral
fixes: Shut breaker five minutes
Dry control box if moisture present
Reseat all harnesses
Check for burned areas on board
when_to_call_technician: Immediate iA0 on every start needs main control replacement.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher iB0 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire iB0 door fault. Adjust strike, clean latch, replace if door shut but code remains.
meaning: iB0 means the door switch circuit failed—related to CL door switch codes on some models.
causes: Misaligned strike
Broken latch hook
Switch lever stuck
Door harness wire broken
fixes: Close door until latch clicks
Clean latch receiver
Inspect strike alignment
Run cycle after firm close
when_to_call_technician: Latch assembly replacement when door appears sealed but iB0 repeats.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher iC0 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire iC0 config fault. Power-cycle, verify correct control board for model.
meaning: iC0 indicates configuration data on the control is invalid or missing after power loss.
causes: Brownout during cycle
Wrong control substituted
Corrupt EEPROM
Incomplete factory flash
fixes: Power off 24 hours after lightning
Match replacement board to model suffix
Reseat UI ribbon
Avoid rapid breaker cycling
when_to_call_technician: Persistent iC0 needs correctly programmed main control.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher iD0 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire iD0 dispenser error. Clean cup hinge, verify wax motor opens mid-wash.
meaning: iD0 means the detergent dispenser did not open on command—SD code family on some units.
causes: Detergent caked in cup
Failed wax motor
Warped dispenser lid
Loose dispenser wire
fixes: Scrape old detergent from cup
Listen for click opening dispenser early in wash
Manually flip lid—it should spring freely
Use fresh pod or gel per instructions
when_to_call_technician: Dispenser motor replacement when door never pops open.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher iE0 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire iE0 soil sensor error. Clean optical sensor lens in sump.
meaning: iE0 indicates the turbidity sensor failed—TU code on related models.
causes: Grease on sensor window
Sensor unplugged after filter service
Cloudy water from excess detergent
Failed sensor module
fixes: Remove lower filter and wipe sensor lens
Reseat sensor harness
Run medium soil load after cleaning
Reduce detergent if water is sudsy
when_to_call_technician: Clean sensor with recurring iE0 needs turbidity sensor assembly.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher iF0 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire iF0 drain restriction. Same fixes as i20/i40—clean filter and air gap.
