meaning: iF0 is grouped with i20 and i40 as a drain restriction code on Frigidaire support charts.
causes: Partially blocked drain hose
Check valve stuck closed
Food debris in sump
Slow household drain
fixes: Clean glass trap and fine filter
Inspect check valve under filter
Verify air gap flows freely
Run cancel-drain twice
when_to_call_technician: Standing water after full cleaning path needs pump service.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher iG0 Error Code
summary: Relay or output driver fault on control
meta_description: Frigidaire iG0 relay fault. One output stuck—heat, valve, or pump may not energize.
meaning: iG0 means a control board output relay failed verification during self-test.
causes: Welded relay contact
Burned driver transistor
Moisture on board
Motor short damaging relay
fixes: Power off and inspect board for burn marks
Identify which function fails—fill, heat, or drain
Do not bypass relays
Note model for control ordering
when_to_call_technician: iG0 requires main control board replacement.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher iH0 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire iH0 firmware fault. Install matched UI and main control from same revision.
meaning: iH0 appears when control firmware versions conflict—often after replacing only one board.
causes: UI replaced without matching main board
Incorrect service flash level
Factory pairing lost
Aftermarket control
fixes: Record serial and existing board numbers
Order UI and control as matched set
Power-cycle after both installed
Avoid mixing boards across model years
when_to_call_technician: Replace both boards together when iH0 follows single-board repair.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator H1 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire H1 freezer thermistor error. Reseat sensor at evaporator, measure ohms.
meaning: H1 on Frigidaire Gallery and Professional refrigerators means the freezer thermistor signal failed.
causes: Sensor unplugged at evaporator cover
Ice encasing sensor bulb
Broken wire in freezer harness
Main board input fault
fixes: Unplug and reseat freezer thermistor connector
Defrost ice around sensor if visible
Compare resistance to Frigidaire chart at room temp
Allow 24 hours stable temps after repair
when_to_call_technician: Correct ohms at plug with persistent H1 needs main control.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator H2 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire H2 refrigerator thermistor error. Check duct sensor clip in fresh food section.
meaning: H2 indicates the refrigerator compartment thermistor failed.
causes: Thermistor pulled from duct clip
Corrosion at board connector
Open sensor wire
Shorted sensor from moisture
fixes: Press sensor fully into airflow duct clip
Reseat harness at main board
Monitor fresh food temps 12 hours
Avoid blocking sensor grille with tall items
when_to_call_technician: Good sensor resistance but H2 needs board or harness service.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator H3 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire H3 fan fault. Clear ice from fan blade, test motor behind freezer panel.
meaning: H3 means the freezer evaporator fan did not report rotation.
causes: Ice bridge blocking fan
Fan motor connector loose
Failed fan bearing
Door switch not closing fan circuit
fixes: Defrost ice at fan shroud with power off
Spin blade by hand when unplugged
Listen for fan 30 seconds after closing door
Verify door switch depresses fully
when_to_call_technician: Fan motor with no spin and clear blades needs 5304517154 or model fan.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator H4 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire H4 damper fault. Listen for damper in fridge ceiling, clear ice blocking door.
meaning: H4 means the motorized damper between freezer and refrigerator did not move.
causes: Ice freezing damper shut
Failed damper motor
Food blocking damper grille
Control not driving motor
fixes: Remove items from upper fridge vent grille
Defrost visible ice at damper housing
Confirm freezer fan runs
Listen for damper motor when door switch released
when_to_call_technician: Damper assembly replacement when motor silent after defrost.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator H5 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire H5 defrost error. Test heater and fuse behind evaporator cover.
meaning: H5 indicates an open defrost heater or defrost circuit failure.
causes: Open defrost heater
Blown defrost thermal fuse
Loose heater connector
Clogged drain causing ice overload
fixes: Remove freezer back panel when unplugged
Test heater continuity
Check defrost fuse in series
Clear drain tube while panel is off
when_to_call_technician: Open heater or fuse needs part replacement and forced defrost test.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator H6 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire H6 defrost thermostat error. Test bimetal on evaporator coil.
meaning: H6 means defrost did not complete correctly—thermostat or timeout related.
causes: Defrost thermostat stuck open
Heavy frost load
Thermistor misreading during defrost
Control defrost algorithm fault
fixes: Manual defrost 24 hours with doors open
Test defrost thermostat continuity cold
Verify heater amps during forced defrost
Clear drain pan tube
when_to_call_technician: H6 after full manual defrost needs thermostat or control service.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator H7 Error Code
summary: Ice maker motor or position fault
meta_description: Frigidaire H7 ice maker error. Clear jammed cubes, align optics, reset feeler arm.
meaning: H7 relates to ice maker harvest or optics feedback failure.
causes: Ice jam in ejector
Optics misaligned
Feeler arm stuck up
Loose connector at ice maker head
fixes: Break up ice bridge in bin
Align emitter and receiver boards
Lower feeler arm to start harvest
Remove stuck cube from ejector
when_to_call_technician: Ice maker module replacement when harvest never completes.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator H8 Error Code
summary: Water valve or dispenser flow fault
meta_description: Frigidaire H8 water system error. Replace filter, test valve at bottom grille.
meaning: H8 indicates water valve, filter restriction, or dispenser circuit fault.
causes: Overdue water filter
Frozen door water line
Dispenser switch stuck
Dual valve not opening
fixes: Install Frigidaire ULTRAWF or listed filter
Dispense two gallons after filter change
Test saddle valve fully open
Listen for valve buzz when paddle pressed
when_to_call_technician: Valve buzz with no water after new filter needs door line thaw or valve.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator H9 Error Code
summary: Compressor start device or relay fault
meta_description: Frigidaire H9 compressor fault. Click-no-run at compressor—test start relay.
meaning: H9 means the compressor did not start when commanded.
causes: Failed PTC start relay
Open overload protector
Loose compressor terminal
Sealed system issue
fixes: Remove lower rear cover
Inspect start relay for burn marks
Shake relay—rattle suggests failure
Listen for click then hum without run
when_to_call_technician: New relay with no run and hot overload needs sealed system tech.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator HA Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire HA door open fault. Close doors fully, check gaskets and switch plungers.
meaning: HA means a door was detected open too long or the switch circuit failed.
causes: Door not fully closed
Warped gasket letting switch flutter
Broken door switch
Heavy items blocking door
fixes: Close both doors firmly
Inspect gasket seal with dollar-bill test
Clean switch plunger area
Relevel doors on French-door models
when_to_call_technician: Switch replacement when doors seal but HA persists.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator HB Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire HB high freezer temp alert. Check fan, coils, door seal—verify compressor runs.
meaning: HB is a high-temperature alarm in the freezer—performance issue rather than sensor-only.
causes: Evaporator fan not running
Heavy frost from defrost failure
Door left open
Weak sealed system
fixes: Confirm compressor and fan run
Defrost if frost blocks coil
Verify gasket seal
Clean condenser coils
when_to_call_technician: HB with running compressor and clear coils needs sealed system diagnosis.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator HC Error Code
summary: High temperature in fresh food section
meta_description: Frigidaire HC refrigerator warm alert. Check damper, fan airflow, thermostat settings.
meaning: HC means fresh food compartment exceeded safe temperature threshold.
causes: Damper stuck closed
Freezer fan failure reducing airflow
Overfilled fridge blocking vents
Control set too warm
fixes: Clear items from vent grilles
Verify damper opens
Set control to recommended mid setting
Allow 24 hours recovery
when_to_call_technician: HC with working fan and open damper needs deeper diagnosis.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator HD Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire HD display error. Reseat ribbon at dispenser UI, dry connectors.
meaning: HD indicates the dispenser or console UI lost communication with the main board.
causes: Loose ribbon at dispenser
Condensation on connector
Surge damage
Door harness chafe
fixes: Unplug and reseat dispenser UI ribbon
Dry any moisture on connectors
Inspect hinge wiring
Test temperature display response
when_to_call_technician: UI board when display dead after harness service.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator HE Error Code
summary: Main control to sub-board link fault
meta_description: Frigidaire HE communication error. Reseat all ceiling and door harnesses.
meaning: HE is a serial communication fault between main control and distributed modules.
causes: Corroded CAN connector
Mismatched control after repair
Intermittent neutral
Failed main UART
fixes: Reseat every harness at main board
Check for combination HD or SY codes
Record model and serial
Avoid mixing board revisions
when_to_call_technician: HE after full harness service needs main control replacement.

title: Frigidaire Refrigerator HF Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire HF system fault. Power-cycle 24h after storm, service if defrost won't complete.
meaning: HF is a combined system fault flag—often defrost performance or firmware integrity on SY/EF platforms.
causes: Repeated defrost failures stacking codes
Corrupt control firmware
Power glitch during update
Failed defrost bi-metal and heater combo
fixes: Unplug 24 hours after lightning
Manual defrost entire freezer
Clear drain pan tube
Run forced defrost if model supports
when_to_call_technician: HF with good defrost parts needs main control with correct firmware.

title: Frigidaire Oven F03 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F03 RTD open. Reseat probe connector, replace sensor if infinite ohms.
meaning: F03 means the oven temperature sensor circuit is open on Frigidaire ranges and wall ovens.
causes: Sensor plug loose
Broken probe wire
Failed RTD
Connector corrosion
fixes: Shut off breaker
Reseat sensor harness behind range
Measure ~1080 ohms at room temp
Replace probe if open
when_to_call_technician: New probe with F03 needs ERC wiring check.

title: Frigidaire Oven F04 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F04 shorted RTD. Inspect probe wire route, replace if near zero ohms.
meaning: F04 indicates a shorted oven sensor—the ERC disables heat.
causes: Wire pinched to cavity
Moisture shorting connector
Failed probe short
Metal touching terminals
fixes: Inspect wire routing through cavity
Dry connector if wet
Replace shorted probe
Retest bake at 350°F
when_to_call_technician: F04 with probe disconnected means harness short.

title: Frigidaire Oven F05 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F05 lock error on self-clean models. Clean latch, test motor movement.
meaning: F05 means the door latch could not lock or unlock for clean or bake.
causes: Grease on latch hook
Failed latch motor
Misaligned door
Broken door switch wire
fixes: Cool oven before testing latch
Clean latch slot and hook
Listen for motor during lock
Verify door closes flush
when_to_call_technician: Latch motor replacement when no shaft movement.

title: Frigidaire Oven F06 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F06 lock switch error. Test microswitches on latch assembly.
meaning: F06 means lock position feedback failed verification.
causes: Switch stuck after clean
Harness unplugged
Failed latch switches
ERC misread
fixes: Move latch manually when cool
Reseat latch harness
Inspect switches on motor
Short bake before self-clean
when_to_call_technician: Latch assembly when switches test bad.

title: Frigidaire Oven F07 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F07 lock motor fault. No voltage response at latch motor.
meaning: F07 targets the lock motor output specifically.
causes: Burned motor windings
ERC relay failure
Mechanical jam
Broken motor gear
fixes: Disconnect power before inspection
Check voltage at motor during lock
Replace latch if motor open
Avoid lubricating unless specified
when_to_call_technician: Motor voltage with no rotation confirms latch replacement.

title: Frigidaire Oven F08 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F08 cooling fan error. Spin fan by hand, reseat harness at rear.
meaning: F08 means the oven cooling fan did not start when required.
causes: Blade obstruction
Open fan motor
Loose connector
ERC fan relay failed
fixes: Power off and spin fan blade
Reseat rear fan harness
Clear vents from storage items
Listen at bake start
when_to_call_technician: Fan motor replacement when voltage present but no spin.

title: Frigidaire Oven F09 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F09 over-temp fault. Shut breaker off until cool—test sensor and ERC relays.
meaning: F09 is a safety shutdown from excessive cavity temperature.
causes: Welded bake relay
Detached sensor reading cold
Runaway clean cycle
External heat near range
fixes: Turn off breaker until cool
Verify sensor mounted correctly
Test element ground leakage
Do not restart until cleared
when_to_call_technician: F09 after cool-down with good sensor suggests ERC replacement.

title: Frigidaire Oven F10 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F10 runaway heat fault. Power off, test bake element and RTD before reuse.
meaning: F10 indicates uncontrolled heating—ERC shuts all outputs.
causes: Shorted bake element to ground
Relay welded on ERC
False cold sensor reading
Broil stuck on
fixes: Disconnect power 30 minutes
Measure element resistance and leakage
Replace erratic sensor first
Inspect broil relay operation
when_to_call_technician: Good parts with immediate F10 on preheat needs ERC.

title: Frigidaire Oven F11 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire oven F11 means a shorted touchpad key. Dry the control panel after spills, clean grease from buttons, and replace the overlay if the short persists.
meaning: F11 means a short on the keypad matrix.
causes: Liquid on panel
Steam shorting membrane
Cracked overlay
ERC input short
fixes: Power off and dry thoroughly
Avoid wet cleaning on touch area
Reseat UI ribbon at ERC
Replace touchpad if short persists unplugged
when_to_call_technician: Touchpad or ERC depending on short location.

title: Frigidaire Oven F12 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F12 stuck key fault. Clean grease around buttons.
meaning: F12 is a stuck-key fault without hard short.
causes: Grease under Start pad
Membrane age
Pressure on panel
Noise interpreted as held key
fixes: Clean and dry all buttons
Press each key to free pad
Breaker off two minutes
Avoid foil contacting door sensor
when_to_call_technician: Touchpad replacement on dry untouched panel.

title: Frigidaire Oven F13 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F13 control memory error. Unplug five minutes, replace ERC if returns.
meaning: F13 means EEPROM checksum failed at power-up.
causes: Surge
Steam on ERC
Age failure
Loose logic supply
fixes: Breaker off five minutes
Inspect for steam damage
Reseat harnesses
Short bake test after reset
when_to_call_technician: Immediate F13 every start needs ERC replacement.

title: Frigidaire Oven F14 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F14 memory data fault. Verify correct ERC part number.
meaning: F14 is secondary EEPROM data corruption on the ERC.
causes: Brownout during clean
Wrong board installed
Bad ground
Memory chip defect
fixes: Match ERC to tech sheet
Tighten mounting screws
Avoid self-clean until fixed
Document serial
when_to_call_technician: Persistent F14 needs new ERC.

title: Frigidaire Oven F15 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F15 internal comm error. Reseat UI ribbon to ERC.
meaning: F15 indicates internal ERC or UI communication failure.
causes: Loose UI ribbon
Failed microcontroller
Static damage
Revision mismatch
fixes: Reseat ribbon both ends
Inspect burnt pins
Match board revisions
Test bake after reseat
when_to_call_technician: F15 after harness needs ERC or touchpad set.

title: Frigidaire Oven F16 Error Code
summary: UI to ERC serial link fault
meta_description: Frigidaire F16 display communication error. Replace matched UI and ERC pair.
meaning: F16 means lost serial link between touch UI and ERC.
causes: Single board swap incompatibility
Cut ribbon
Corroded comm pins
Failed UART driver
fixes: Order UI and ERC together
Replace torn ribbon
Run keypad test if available
Power-cycle after work
when_to_call_technician: Matched pair when F16 follows one-board repair.

title: Frigidaire Oven F17 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F17 probe error. Seat probe fully, check jack wiring to ERC.
meaning: F17 appears on probe-equipped models when the meat probe circuit is open or shorted.
causes: Probe not fully inserted
Damaged probe cable
Loose jack wires
Damaged probe tip
fixes: Insert probe until seated
Test with known-good probe
Inspect jack behind console
Use timed bake as workaround
when_to_call_technician: Good probe with F17 at jack needs receptacle or ERC repair.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F2 Error Code
summary: Stuck button on the user interface
meta_description: Kenmore washer F2 means a keypad button is stuck. Wipe the panel dry, power-cycle, replace UI if a key reads pressed constantly.
meaning: F2 on Kenmore Elite HE front-load washers (110.41382, 110.41952 series) means the Central Control Unit sees a user-interface button held down. The washer will not accept cycle changes until the stuck key clears.
causes: Liquid detergent or bleach residue bridging touch pads
Cracked membrane overlay shorting adjacent buttons
Moisture in the console after a spill
Failed user-interface board behind the control panel
fixes: Wipe the control surface with a barely damp cloth and dry thoroughly
Unplug two minutes, restore power, and press each button once
Avoid pressing multiple keys during power-up on HE3t and HE5t models
Replace the UI assembly if F2 appears with no keys touched
when_to_call_technician: Order the UI by full model number from the rating plate if F2 persists on a dry, untouched panel.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F3 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore washer F3 SCC short fault. Inspect pressure hose from tub dome to CCU, check for water in the air tube.
meaning: F3 means the Suds Control Circuit or pressure transducer on the Central Control Unit reads a short. Water level detection fails and the washer may stop fill or drain steps immediately.
causes: Pressure hose pushed too far onto the CCU stem causing an air leak
Water trapped inside the clear pressure tube from a prior overflow
Pinched hose between the tub ring and cabinet
Failed pressure transducer integrated into the CCU
fixes: Remove the pressure hose and blow through it to confirm it is open
Reattach with a snug fit without bottoming the hose on the board stem
Inspect the tub air dome port for detergent crust
Run a short fill cycle and listen for normal water level changes
when_to_call_technician: A clear, intact hose with recurring F3 during normal fill requires CCU replacement—the transducer is not field-serviceable.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F4 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore washer F4 pressure switch fault on drain. Check air dome hose, run Spin/Drain, verify pump removes water.
meaning: F4 appears when the pressure switch does not release during the drain portion of a cycle. The CCU still sees tub water when the drain pump should have emptied the drum.
causes: Clogged drain pump filter slowing extraction
Kinked drain hose behind the washer
Pressure hose disconnected at the tub dome
Failed drain pump not moving water
fixes: Open the lower front access panel and twist out the pump clean-out filter
Straighten the drain hose and confirm the standpipe drains freely
Verify the pressure hose is firmly seated at the tub air dome
Run Spin/Drain and watch whether water exits the emergency hose
when_to_call_technician: Standing water after Spin/Drain with a clear filter and hose points to a failed drain pump.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F5 Error Code
summary: Water temperature sensor out of range
meta_description: Kenmore washer F5 NTC fault. Check inlet thermistor wiring, verify hot and cold hoses, test sensor resistance.
meaning: F5 means the water temperature sensor (NTC) on Kenmore HE washers reported a value outside the expected range during the water heating step. Heated cycles may abort before reaching target temperature.
causes: Hot and cold fill hoses reversed at the inlet valve
Open or shorted NTC wire at the mixing valve
No hot water supply to the hot inlet port
Failed thermistor inside the water inlet assembly
fixes: Trace hoses—cold should connect to the blue valve, hot to red
Run the laundry tap and confirm hot water is warm before starting
Unplug and reseat the NTC harness at the inlet valve
Measure NTC resistance against the chart in the tech sheet behind the lower panel
when_to_call_technician: Correct hose routing with lukewarm tap water that still triggers F5 needs inlet valve and thermistor replacement.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F6 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore washer F6 door lock fault. Clear gasket obstructions, check door tab, test lock motor at lower front access.
meaning: F6 means the door lock assembly could not achieve a locked position after multiple attempts. Kenmore HE front-loaders block fill and spin until the latch confirms the door is secure.
causes: Clothing trapped in the door boot fold
Broken door tab that inserts into the lock receiver
Failed door lock motor or wax motor
Loose three-wire harness at the lock assembly
fixes: Pull fabric out of the gasket crease and press the door until it clicks
Inspect the door tab for cracks or wear
Unplug, open the lower access panel, and reseat the lock harness
Listen for lock motor buzz at cycle start—silence with F6 means replace the lock
when_to_call_technician: A new door lock with intact wiring that still shows F6 may indicate CCU lock driver failure.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F7 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore washer F7 MCU processor error. Reseat MS2 harness at CCU, inspect motor control board for burn marks.
meaning: F7 means the Motor Control Unit detected an internal processor fault. The drive system stops and the drum will not agitate or spin until the MCU resets or is replaced.
causes: Power surge damaging the MCU processor
Loose MS2 harness between CCU and MCU at the top rear
Moisture on the MCU from a prior inlet leak
Failed MCU EEPROM or drive processor
fixes: Unplug five minutes and restore power
Remove the top cover and reseat the MS2 connector at the CCU
Inspect the MCU mounted on the left cabinet wall for burn marks
Run a short spin cycle as a functional test after harness reseat
when_to_call_technician: Persistent F7 after reseating MS2 and M13 connectors requires a new Motor Control Unit matched to your model suffix.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F8 Error Code
summary: Water inlet valve stuck open or closed
meta_description: Kenmore washer F8 water valve fault. Turn off supply taps, test valves for continuous fill, replace inlet valve if stuck.
meaning: F8 means the CCU detected a water inlet valve that would not close or would not open on command. Fill may stop mid-cycle or continue when the washer is powered off.
causes: Inlet valve solenoid stuck open from mineral buildup
Failed valve not opening when commanded to fill
Loose spade connectors at the dual inlet valve
CCU relay welded on the water valve circuit
fixes: Shut off hot and cold taps behind the washer immediately if tub fills when off
Unplug and confirm whether water still enters the drum with power removed
Inspect valve wire terminals for corrosion
Replace the dual inlet valve if water flows with the unit unplugged
when_to_call_technician: Call service before running another fill cycle if the tub fills while unplugged—both solenoids are stuck open.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F9 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore washer F9 overflow fault. Shut off water, check inlet valves and pressure hose, drain standing water.
meaning: F9 reports an overflow condition on Kenmore HE front-load washers. The pressure switch sees tub water above the safe fill level and the drain pump may run continuously while the cycle halts.
causes: Inlet valve stuck open filling when the washer is off
Pressure switch air dome hose cracked or disconnected
Drain pump unable to remove water during a fill phase
Excess suds from non-HE detergent raising perceived water level
fixes: Turn off hot and cold supply valves behind the washer
Inspect the clear pressure hose from the tub dome to the CCU
Run Drain/Spin only with water supply off until F9 clears
Switch to HE detergent at the lowest marked dose if suds were visible
when_to_call_technician: F9 with a tub that fills while unplugged requires inlet valve replacement before any further use.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F10 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore washer F10 MCU memory fault. Power-cycle, reseat MS2 harness—replace motor control board if code returns.
meaning: F10 means the Motor Control Unit failed its EEPROM memory check. Drive commands from the CCU are ignored until the MCU resets or is replaced.
causes: Lightning surge through the laundry circuit
Loose MS2 communication harness after moving the washer
MCU board moisture from a vent leak
Corrupted EEPROM on the motor control assembly
fixes: Unplug the washer for five minutes
Reseat the MS2 connector at both CCU and MCU ends
Inspect MCU for swollen capacitors or burnt traces
Run a short spin after power returns as a board test
when_to_call_technician: F10 that returns immediately after power-up requires a new Motor Control Unit with matching part number.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F11 Error Code
summary: Communication fault between CCU and MCU
meta_description: Kenmore washer F11 CCU-to-MCU comm error. Reseat MS2 and M13 connectors, check wiring diagram in lower panel packet.
meaning: F11 is the most common Kenmore HE4t and HE5t fault—it means serial data between the Central Control Unit and Motor Control Unit was lost. Rinse and spin may stop mid-cycle when the boards cannot exchange motor commands.
causes: Loose MS2(1,2,3) connector at the CCU after vibration
Corroded pins on the M13 harness at the MCU
Failed CCU serial driver chip
Failed MCU not acknowledging CCU messages
fixes: Unplug and remove the top cover to access connectors labeled MS2 and M13 on the tech diagram
Remove and reseat both harness ends—squeeze female pin gaps if loose
Power-cycle five minutes after reconnecting
Run the same cycle that triggered F11 as a verification test
when_to_call_technician: F11 after reseating both harnesses usually requires CCU or MCU replacement—order by model suffix from the rating plate.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F12 Error Code
summary: Dispenser motor cannot reach latch position
meta_description: Kenmore washer F12 dispenser fault. Check detergent drawer for blockage, inspect dispenser motor and cam.
meaning: F12 means the detergent dispenser motor could not drive the diverter cam to the commanded position. Auto-dispense cycles on Kenmore HE models may skip bleach or fabric-softener steps.
causes: Caked detergent blocking the dispenser drawer slides
Broken cam on the dispenser motor gearbox
Dispenser motor harness unplugged behind the top panel
Failed dispenser motor winding
fixes: Remove the dispenser drawer and rinse all compartments with warm water
Cycle the manual dispenser lever by hand to confirm free movement
Unplug and reseat the dispenser motor plug at the top rear
Run a cycle using each dispenser compartment to test operation
when_to_call_technician: Replace the dispenser motor assembly if the cam does not advance when voltage is present during service diagnostics.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F13 Error Code
summary: Dispenser motor current out of range
meta_description: Kenmore washer F13 dispenser current fault. Clear drawer blockage, test dispenser motor amp draw, replace motor if stalled.
meaning: F13 means the CCU measured abnormal current from the dispenser motor—either too high from a mechanical stall or too low from an open winding.
causes: Detergent buildup binding the dispenser cam
Foreign object jammed in the dispenser housing
Shorted dispenser motor winding
Open wire in the dispenser circuit
fixes: Clean hardened detergent from the drawer tracks and rear inlet ports
Manually rotate the dispenser cam—it should move without grinding
Inspect the motor harness for pinched wires at the top panel hinge
Replace the dispenser motor if the cam is free but F13 persists
when_to_call_technician: New dispenser motor with immediate F13 may indicate a CCU current sense fault.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F14 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore washer F14 CCU memory fault. Unplug five minutes, reseat harnesses—replace Central Control Unit if fault returns.
meaning: F14 means the Central Control Unit detected a corrupted EEPROM or memory verification failure. Cycle parameters cannot be stored reliably until the board is reset or replaced.
causes: Power surge corrupting CCU memory
Loose 14-pin harness at the CCU after console service
Moisture on the board from an inlet valve leak
Failed EEPROM chip on the main control assembly
fixes: Unplug five minutes to discharge board capacitors
Remove the top cover and reseat every harness into the CCU
Inspect for burn marks near the line input on the CCU
Restore power and run a short rinse as a functional test
when_to_call_technician: Persistent F14 after harness reseat requires a new Kenmore Central Control Unit matched to your model suffix.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F15 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore washer F15 CCU fault. Power-cycle, inspect CCU for moisture damage, replace board if code is immediate on boot.
meaning: F15 is a secondary CCU memory fault on some Kenmore HE platforms—the control cannot pass its startup integrity check and may flash F15 before any cycle will start.
causes: Repeated power interruptions during a cycle
CCU connector corrosion from steam at the door vent
Manufacturing EEPROM defect on older CCU revisions
Water drip onto the CCU from a leaking dispenser
fixes: Unplug for ten minutes before restoring power
Dry the CCU area if any moisture is visible on the mounting panel
Reseat the large main harness and UI ribbon at the CCU
Record the code and serial number before ordering a replacement board
when_to_call_technician: F15 displayed within seconds of power-on requires CCU replacement—do not attempt live board repair.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F16 Error Code
summary: Door lock cannot lock or unlock
meta_description: Kenmore washer F16 door lock cycle fault. Test lock motor, inspect wax motor, replace door lock if latch won't cycle.
meaning: F16 means the door lock mechanism failed to complete a lock or unlock cycle. The CCU commanded the latch but did not receive the expected position feedback within the timeout window.
causes: Broken lock cam inside the door latch assembly
Wax motor not advancing the latch hook
Door boot interference preventing latch engagement
Lock position switch failed inside the assembly
fixes: Unplug and manually check that the door tab aligns with the latch receiver
Open the lower access panel and listen for lock motor movement on power-up
Inspect the lock wiring for broken conductors at the door hinge area
Replace the complete door lock assembly if the motor runs without latching
when_to_call_technician: F16 with a new lock assembly and intact wiring may require CCU lock driver service.

title: Kenmore Dryer F01 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F01 EEPROM fault on main control. Unplug, reseat harnesses, replace board if code returns on power-up.
meaning: F01 on Kenmore 110-series dryers means the main electronic control failed its internal memory check. Heat, motor, and moisture-sensing outputs stop until the board resets or is replaced.
causes: Lightning surge through the laundry circuit
Loose main harness connector after moving the dryer
Moisture corrosion on the board from a vent leak
Failed EEPROM on the electronic control assembly
fixes: Unplug the dryer for five minutes before restoring power
Remove the top panel and reseat the large harness into the control board
Inspect for burnt traces near the line input terminals
Run a timed dry on air fluff as a board function test
when_to_call_technician: F01 that returns immediately after power-up requires a new Kenmore electronic control matched to your model suffix.

title: Kenmore Dryer F02 Error Code
summary: Key stuck on the user interface
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F02 stuck button fault. Wipe console dry, power-cycle, replace UI if keys read pressed constantly.
meaning: F02 means the console detects a keypad or touch button held continuously. The Kenmore dryer will not start a cycle while it thinks a selection key is pressed.
causes: Static discharge locking a touch pad trace
Spilled liquid on the console membrane
Cracked overlay shorting adjacent pads
Failed user interface board behind the console
fixes: Wipe the console dry with a soft cloth
Unplug two minutes and touch each button once after restart
Avoid leaning on the console during operation
Replace the UI if F02 shows with no keys touched
when_to_call_technician: UI board replacement requires matching the part to your console style—order by model number from the rating plate.

title: Kenmore Dryer F03 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F03 exhaust temperature sensor fault. Reseat thermistor at blower housing, vacuum lint from connector.
meaning: F03 means the exhaust thermistor sent an open-circuit reading. The control cannot modulate heat safely without exhaust temperature feedback and may shut off the burner or element.
causes: Two-wire thermistor connector loose at the blower housing
Broken wire in the harness near the drum bulkhead
Failed thermistor element internally open
Heavy lint shorting the sensor connector
fixes: Unplug and reseat the thermistor plug on the blower outlet
Vacuum lint from the connector area
Measure thermistor resistance against the service chart at room temperature
Run a timed cycle on low heat after wiring is verified
when_to_call_technician: Open thermistor at room temp with intact wiring requires sensor or harness replacement.

title: Kenmore Dryer F04 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F04 moisture bar fault. Clean sensor strips inside drum, check wiring to control board.
meaning: F04 indicates the moisture sensor circuit is open. Auto-dry cycles on Kenmore dryers rely on the two metal strips inside the front bulkhead to detect dampness and end the cycle.
causes: Sensor wire unplugged behind the front panel
Coating of fabric softener insulating the bars
Corroded sensor terminals from years of use
Open circuit in the sensor harness
fixes: Clean the two metal moisture bars inside the drum with rubbing alcohol
Remove front bulkhead access and verify the sensor harness is plugged in
Inspect for broken wires where the door harness flexes
Run Sensor Dry on a small damp load as a functional test
when_to_call_technician: Persistent F04 with clean bars and seated wiring needs moisture sensor harness or board service.

title: Kenmore Dryer F05 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F05 door switch fault. Confirm door clicks closed, test switch continuity, replace if drum won't run.
meaning: F05 means the door switch never reported closed. Kenmore dryers block motor and heat circuits until the door strike depresses the switch plunger fully.
causes: Worn door catch not pressing the switch
Broken door switch lever
Door hinge sag misaligning the strike
Failed door switch internally open
fixes: Close the door firmly and listen for the drum light to extinguish
Inspect the switch plunger through the front panel gap
Replace the door switch if the drum won't turn with the door fully shut
Check hinge screws for looseness causing misalignment
when_to_call_technician: Door switch replacement is a common DIY repair—call service only if wiring inside the door is damaged.

title: Kenmore Dryer F06 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F06 UI-to-control comm error. Reseat console harness ribbon, inspect for corrosion.
meaning: F06 means the user interface board and main control lost serial communication. Display, knob input, and heat commands cannot synchronize on Kenmore electronic-console dryers.
causes: Loose ribbon harness between console and main board
Power surge damaging communication chips
Corrosion on the UI connector from laundry humidity
Failed UI or main control not acknowledging signals
fixes: Unplug and remove the console to reseat the UI harness
Inspect ribbon cable for creases or pin damage
Power-cycle five minutes after reconnecting
Test with a basic timed dry cycle
when_to_call_technician: F06 after reseating both ends of the UI harness usually requires UI board replacement first.

title: Kenmore Dryer F07 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F07 shorted moisture sensor. Clean bars, dry harness, replace sensor strip if short persists.
meaning: F07 is the opposite of F04—the moisture sensor circuit reads a short. Auto-dry may end immediately or not advance because the board sees constant wetness.
causes: Metal debris bridging the two sensor bars
Water splash on the sensor harness during cleaning
Shorted wires where harness rubs the cabinet
Failed sensor strip with internal short
fixes: Clean and dry the moisture bars inside the drum
Inspect the harness behind the bulkhead for pinched insulation
Disconnect the sensor plug and measure for short to ground
Run a timed dry instead of auto-dry until repaired
when_to_call_technician: Sensor circuit tests good but F07 remains with the harness disconnected—replace the sensor assembly.

title: Kenmore Dryer F08 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F08 static on user interface. Touch Start again after discharge; humidify dry laundry rooms.
meaning: F08 is a static-sensitive keypad fault on Kenmore dryers. Dry winter air or synthetic clothing can discharge into the touch panel and momentarily confuse the UI.
causes: Very low humidity in the laundry room
Static buildup from synthetic loads
Ungrounded outlet missing equipment ground
Sensitive UI board without discharge protection
fixes: Touch a grounded metal surface before pressing dryer buttons
Unplug one minute and retry the cycle selection
Avoid dragging feet on carpet before using the console
Install a humidifier nearby if F08 is frequent in winter
when_to_call_technician: F08 on every start with a verified grounded outlet may need UI board replacement.

title: Kenmore Dryer F09 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F09 incompatible software between UI and control. Both boards may need matched replacement pair.
meaning: F09 appears when the user interface and main control report mismatched software versions. After a partial board replacement the two assemblies may not communicate correctly.
causes: UI board replaced without matching main control revision
Main control replaced with incompatible firmware level
Factory flash corruption on one board
Aftermarket control not paired to the console
fixes: Record model and serial numbers from the rating plate
Verify both boards carry the same engineering revision suffix
Power-cycle after any single-board swap
Contact Sears PartsDirect with the serial to order a matched pair
when_to_call_technician: F09 after replacing either board usually requires installing the manufacturer-matched UI and control set together.

title: Kenmore Dryer F10 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F10 power supply fault. Test outlet for 120V, check breaker and cord terminal block.
meaning: F10 means the control detected line voltage outside the safe 108–132 VAC window. Motor and heat outputs are disabled to protect components.
causes: Loose cord at the terminal block
Shared circuit with heavy loads causing voltage sag
Tripped or weak breaker contact
Incorrect outlet wiring with lost neutral
fixes: Test the outlet with a multimeter under load
Tighten the power cord strain relief and terminal screws
Move the dryer to a dedicated 20-amp laundry circuit if possible
Reset the breaker fully off then on
when_to_call_technician: Voltage below 108 VAC at the terminal block with a good outlet indicates house wiring service, not the dryer.

title: Kenmore Dryer F11 Error Code
summary: Motor relay stuck on the control board
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F11 motor relay fault. Unplug immediately if drum runs with door open, replace main control.
meaning: F11 means the main control detected a motor relay that would not release. The drum may continue turning when the cycle should be paused—a safety concern on Kenmore electronic dryers.
causes: Welded motor relay contacts on the control board
Shorted motor winding keeping relay energized
Failed relay driver transistor
Moisture bridging relay pins on the board
fixes: Unplug the dryer immediately if the drum runs with the door open
Inspect the motor relay area on the control for heat discoloration
Test motor winding resistance for shorts to ground
Do not use the dryer until F11 is cleared
when_to_call_technician: F11 with a good motor requires main control board replacement—relay repair is not practical in the field.

title: Kenmore Dryer F12 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore gas dryer F12 valve fault. Check gas supply, test valve coils, inspect radiant sensor on burner.
meaning: F12 on Kenmore gas dryers means the gas valve circuit did not respond correctly. The burner will not ignite and clothes remain wet after a full cycle.
causes: Gas supply valve behind the dryer turned off
Failed gas valve coil not opening on heat call
Open radiant flame sensor circuit
Loose wire at the gas valve harness
fixes: Confirm the gas shutoff behind the dryer is fully open
Listen for valve coil click at heat start—silence means test coils
Inspect the radiant sensor for soot coating
Verify other gas appliances in the home work
when_to_call_technician: Gas valve and burner service requires a qualified technician—do not disassemble gas lines yourself.

title: Kenmore Dryer F13 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F13 lint screen sensor fault. Clean screen housing contacts, reseat harness at console.
meaning: F13 on select Kenmore dryers means the lint screen presence sensor circuit failed. Some models use this input to verify the lint filter is installed before heat is enabled.
causes: Lint packed around the screen housing contacts
Screen not fully seated in the channel
Broken sensor wire at the filter housing
Failed microswitch in the lint screen pocket
fixes: Remove and wash the lint screen—vacuum the housing channel
Confirm the screen drops fully into place with an audible seat
Inspect the two contact strips in the housing for corrosion
Reseat the sensor harness behind the console
when_to_call_technician: F13 with a clean, seated screen and good contacts needs the lint housing sensor or harness replaced.

title: Kenmore Dryer F14 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore electric dryer F14 heat relay fault. Unplug if heat runs continuously, test element, replace control board.
meaning: F14 means the main control detected a heating element relay that would not open. The dryer may overheat or continue heating when the cycle should be in cool-down.
causes: Welded heat relay on the electronic control
Shorted heating element leaking to ground
Failed relay driver on the board
Loose element wire causing erratic current draw
fixes: Unplug immediately if the drum is hot with no cycle running
Measure element resistance and check for ground leakage
Inspect the control board relay area for burn marks
Do not run heated cycles until F14 is diagnosed
when_to_call_technician: Good element with persistent F14 requires main control board replacement.

title: Kenmore Dryer F15 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F15 shorted exhaust thermistor. Inspect blower housing wiring, replace thermistor if shorted.
meaning: F15 means the exhaust thermistor reads a short circuit. The control disables heat because it cannot trust exhaust temperature feedback.
causes: Thermistor wires pinched at the blower housing
Water from a vent leak shorting the connector
Failed thermistor with internal short
Metal screw through harness insulation
fixes: Unplug and inspect thermistor wiring along the blower outlet
Dry the connector if moisture is present
Measure resistance—near zero ohms at room temp confirms a short
Replace the thermistor if wiring is intact
when_to_call_technician: New thermistor with immediate F15 may indicate a control board input fault.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F2 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore 665 dishwasher F2 leak fault. Dry base pan, check door gasket, find leak source before restarting.
meaning: F2 on Whirlpool-built Kenmore dishwashers (665.xxxxx) means water reached the base pan and tripped the anti-flood float switch. The control stops filling and may run the drain pump continuously.
causes: Torn or compressed door gasket letting spray escape
Loose drain hose clamp dripping into the pan
Regular dish soap instead of automatic detergent causing suds overflow
Worn tub seal at the pump allowing wash water to leak down
fixes: Turn off power and water, then tilt the unit forward slightly to check the base pan
Soak up water with towels and dry the pan completely
Inspect the door gasket for tears—replace WPW10300924 if damaged
Run a cycle while watching underneath for the active leak point
when_to_call_technician: Persistent internal leaks from pump seals or tub welds require pulling the dishwasher for component access.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F3 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F3 thermistor error. Test NTC at sump, clean connector, replace WPW10467289 sensor.
meaning: F3 means the tub water thermistor is reading abnormally—open or shorted. Kenmore 665 dishwashers use this NTC near the heating element to regulate wash and dry temperatures.
causes: Failed NTC thermistor WPW10467289
Disconnected sensor wire in the tub sump area
Sensor corroded from hard water deposits on the pins
Connector loose at the electronic control board
fixes: Access the thermistor mounted near the heating element under the tub
Measure resistance—about 55K ohms at 77°F, dropping near 20K at 120°F
Clean corroded connector pins with electrical contact cleaner
Replace the sensor if reading is 0 or infinite
when_to_call_technician: New sensor with correct resistance but recurring F3 may indicate main control board input failure.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F4 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F4 circulating pump fault. Check for glass in impeller, test motor windings, reseat harness.
meaning: F4 means the main wash motor did not start or did not reach expected speed. Spray arms will not rotate and dishes stay dirty after the wash phase times out.
causes: Glass shard or seed lodged in the wash impeller
Failed wash motor capacitor on single-phase motors
Loose harness at the motor under the tub
Seized motor bearings from hard water scale
fixes: Disconnect power and remove the lower spray arm and filter cover
Spin the impeller by hand—it should turn freely
Reseat the two-wire or three-wire motor harness at the pump body
Listen for motor hum during wash—silence with F4 means replace the motor
when_to_call_technician: Motor winding open or seized bearings require wash pump motor replacement.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F5 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F5 soil sensor error. Clean optical sensor in sump, check wiring, replace turbidity assembly.
meaning: F5 means the turbidity or soil sensor in the sump reported an invalid reading. Auto-wash cycles on newer Kenmore 665 models use this sensor to adjust cycle length based on water clarity.
causes: Grease film coating the optical sensor lens
Failed turbidity sensor sending constant high soil signal
Wiring open between sensor and control board
Cloudy rinse aid residue blocking the light path
fixes: Remove the lower filter and wipe the soil sensor window with a soft cloth
Run a cup of white vinegar through an empty cycle to dissolve grease film
Reseat the sensor harness at the sump
Replace the turbidity sensor if readings stay fixed after cleaning
when_to_call_technician: New turbidity sensor with immediate F5 may indicate control board analog input failure.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F6 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F6 inlet fault. Open supply valve, clean inlet screen, test WPW10195049 valve.
meaning: F6 means the dishwasher did not fill with water within the allowed time. The inlet valve never opened or household pressure was too low for the fill step.
causes: Water supply valve under the sink turned off or partially closed
Kinked inlet hose between valve and dishwasher
Clogged inlet valve screen with mineral deposits
Faulty water inlet valve WPW10195049
fixes: Turn the shut-off valve under the sink fully counterclockwise
Inspect the inlet hose for kinks behind the kick plate
Disconnect the hose at the valve and clean the mesh screen with vinegar
Listen for water entering the tub at fill start—silence means test or replace the valve
when_to_call_technician: Clean screen with adequate household pressure but no fill requires inlet valve replacement.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F7 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F7 flow meter fault. Clean turbine housing, test WPW10348269 meter, verify inlet valve opens.
meaning: F7 means the flow meter turbine did not pulse when the inlet valve opened. The control uses this sensor to verify water is actually entering the tub—not just that the valve coil energized.
causes: Mineral buildup blocking the flow meter turbine impeller
Failed flow meter WPW10348269
Inlet valve opening only partially
Debris in the inlet path upstream of the meter
fixes: Remove the flow meter from the inlet valve assembly and rinse the turbine
Blow through the meter housing to confirm the impeller spins freely
Verify full water flow at the inlet hose when the valve is commanded open
Replace the flow meter if the turbine is clear but no pulse is detected
when_to_call_technician: New flow meter and valve with persistent F7 may indicate control board fill logic failure.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F8 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F8 drain fault. Clean filter, check disposal knockout, test drain pump motor.
meaning: F8 means the drain pump ran but water remained in the tub after the drain timeout. Standing water at the end of a cycle or cancel/drain triggers this code on Kenmore 665 dishwashers.
causes: Clogged food filter at the tub bottom
Blocked drain hose at the disposal or air gap
Failed drain pump motor
Garbage disposal knockout plug still installed on new setups
fixes: Remove the lower spray arm and clean the filter assembly thoroughly
Check the drain hose at the disposal—remove knockout plug if recently installed
Verify the drain hose has a high loop or air gap
Listen at drain—humming with no water movement means jammed or failed pump
when_to_call_technician: Clear filter and hose with a silent pump at drain command requires drain pump replacement.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F9 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F9 diverter fault. Clear debris from diverter disc, test WPW10195677 motor.
meaning: F9 means the diverter motor that routes water between upper and lower spray arms did not reach the commanded position. One rack may get no spray during the affected portion of the cycle.
causes: Food debris jamming the diverter disc at the tub bottom
Failed diverter motor WPW10195677
Wiring fault between motor and control board
Broken diverter cam not indexing spray ports
fixes: Remove the lower spray arm and filter to access the diverter area
Clear hard particles or seeds from the diverter disc track
Test motor resistance—should read 1,000–3,000 ohms on many models
Replace the diverter motor if the disc is clear but F9 persists
when_to_call_technician: New diverter motor with immediate F9 may indicate control board motor driver failure.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F10 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F10 vent fan fault. Check fan at door vent, clear obstruction, test fan motor circuit.
meaning: F10 means the drying vent fan did not start when commanded. Kenmore dishwashers with a vent fan use it to pull moist air out during the dry phase—F10 leaves dishes wet and may trip related heat faults.
causes: Lint or debris blocking the vent fan blade
Failed vent fan motor winding
Loose connector at the vent assembly in the door
Stuck vent door flap preventing fan airflow
fixes: Remove the vent grille on the door and inspect the fan blade
Spin the fan by hand when power is off—it should rotate freely
Reseat the vent fan harness behind the inner door panel
Run a heated dry cycle and listen for fan start within 30 seconds
when_to_call_technician: Fan motor that won't run with good voltage at the connector needs vent assembly replacement.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F11 Error Code
summary: Touchpad cannot talk to main control
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F11 UI comm error. Reseat ribbon cable at door, clean P1C connector pins.
meaning: F11 means the touchpad user interface lost communication with the main control board inside the door. The display may freeze or alternate between F11 and blank segments.
causes: Loose ribbon cable between UI and main control at P1C
Corroded connector pins from steam through the door vent
Failed user interface board
Main control UI port no longer providing 5 VDC
fixes: Flip the breaker off and remove inner door screws to access the harness
Unplug and reseat the UI ribbon at the main control P1C connector
Clean green corrosion on pins with electrical contact cleaner
Restore power after one minute off—immediate F11 means plan board replacement
when_to_call_technician: A technician can measure 5 VDC at P1C before you buy a UI or main board for F11.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F12 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F12 dispenser fault. Free stuck dispenser lid, test wax motor or solenoid, replace dispenser.
meaning: F12 means the detergent dispenser did not open during the wash step. Dishes may come out greasy when the main wash detergent never entered the tub.
causes: Detergent caked in the dispenser lid hinge
Failed wax motor or solenoid on the dispenser
Broken dispenser latch spring
Loose wire at the dispenser actuator
fixes: Open the dispenser lid manually and rinse hardened detergent from the hinge
Run a cycle and listen for a click at the wash fill step
Inspect the dispenser actuator wiring behind the door panel
Replace the dispenser assembly if the lid won't pop open on command
when_to_call_technician: New dispenser with no actuator movement during service test needs control board output diagnosis.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F13 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F13 memory fault. Power-cycle, reseat door harness—replace main control if immediate on boot.
meaning: F13 means the main electronic control failed its EEPROM memory verification. Cycle programs cannot load reliably and the dishwasher may not start any cycle.
causes: Power surge corrupting control board memory
Moisture on the board from a door gasket leak
Loose main harness after door service
Failed EEPROM on the electronic control assembly
fixes: Flip the breaker off for five minutes
Reseat all harness connectors on the main control inside the door
Inspect for burn marks or swollen capacitors on the board
Record model number before ordering a replacement control
when_to_call_technician: F13 displayed within seconds of power-on requires main control board replacement.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F14 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F14 touchpad fault. Clean panel, test each button, replace UI overlay or assembly.
meaning: F14 means the touchpad membrane or button panel failed self-test. Button presses may not register or the panel may report multiple keys at once.
causes: Touch panel membrane degraded after years of use
Moisture under the overlay from steam migration
Ribbon cable corrosion at the UI connector
Physical damage to the button overlay
fixes: Press each button firmly and release—note any that feel sticky
Dry the control surface and avoid spraying cleaner directly on buttons
Disconnect and clean the ribbon cable contacts
Replace the touchpad overlay or combined UI assembly if buttons don't respond
when_to_call_technician: UI replacement on built-in models requires matching the door panel style to your series.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F15 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F15 heater fault. Test element resistance, check relay on control, inspect terminals.
meaning: F15 means the heating element circuit did not draw expected current during heat step. Wash water stays cold and the dry phase fails on Kenmore 665 dishwashers.
causes: Open heating element under the tub
Loose spade connector at the heater terminals
Welded heater relay on the control board
Scale bridging heater terminals causing abnormal current
fixes: Disconnect power and remove the lower access panel to reach heater terminals
Measure element resistance—most read between 10 and 50 ohms
Inspect relay area on the control for heat discoloration
Run a heated wash and feel the tub water warming at the end of main wash
when_to_call_technician: Good element with recurring F15 requires control board relay or triac replacement.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F16 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F16 door switch fault. Confirm door latches fully, adjust strike, replace switch if open.
meaning: F16 means the door switch circuit reads open when the control expects the door to be latched. The dishwasher will not fill or run wash steps until the switch confirms closure.
causes: Door not fully latched against the tub gasket
Warped door strike not depressing the switch
Failed door switch microswitch
Broken wire in the door latch harness
fixes: Close the door firmly until the latch clicks
Inspect the strike plate alignment on the tub frame
Test door switch continuity with the door latched
Replace the door latch assembly if the switch stays open when latched
when_to_call_technician: Door latch replacement is straightforward—call service if the harness inside the door is damaged.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 22
summary: Pantry compartment sensor 2 out of range
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 22 means PC sensor 2 resistance fault. Reseat module connector, test cold-side thermistor.
meaning: Code 22 on Kenmore 106-series refrigerators means pantry compartment sensor 2 resistance is outside the normal range—above 45K ohms when cold. The ice maker module cannot read accurate temperature for the refrigerated drawer zone.
causes: Loose connector at the ice maker or pantry module
Open-circuit PC2 thermistor wire in the door harness
Failed PC2 sensor reading too high when chilled
Corroded pins at the module J1 connector from condensation
fixes: Enter service diagnostics per your tech sheet and note whether 22 appears in Test 56
Unplug the refrigerator and reseat the PC sensor harness at the module
Measure PC2 sensor resistance at the connector against the cold-temperature spec
Replace the PC2 thermistor if resistance stays above 45K ohms at refrigerator temperature
when_to_call_technician: Module replacement may be required if the sensor tests good but code 22 persists in diagnostics.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 23
summary: Ice maker switch not cycling during harvest
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 23 means ice maker switch fault. Check harvest cam, test switch continuity, clear ice jam.
meaning: Code 23 means the board did not see the ice maker switch cycle during the harvest rotation. The harvest cam should momentarily open the switch at home position—without that pulse the module thinks the gearbox is out of sync.
causes: Ice jam preventing the ejector from reaching home
Broken switch actuator tab on the ice maker gearbox
Failed microswitch inside the ice maker head
Loose wire at the switch harness on the module
fixes: Remove the ice bin and inspect for cubes blocking the ejector rake
Manually rotate the ice maker cam through one harvest using the test jumper per the tech sheet
Listen for a click as the cam passes the switch home position
Replace the ice maker assembly if the cam moves but no switch click is heard
when_to_call_technician: Ice maker module and gearbox replacement is easiest through service if manual harvest does not produce a switch pulse.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 24
summary: Ice tray sensor resistance too high
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 24 means ice tray sensor open/high. Test tray thermistor, check module wiring, replace sensor.
meaning: Code 24 means the ice tray thermistor resistance is above the normal upper limit—greater than about 45K ohms. The module cannot determine mold temperature for freeze and harvest timing.
causes: Open ice tray thermistor wire in the ice maker harness
Failed thermistor inside the ice mold body
Connector unplugged at the ice maker head
Ice buildup insulating the sensor from the mold metal
fixes: Run Service Test 58 on models with ice maker diagnostics to view tray thermistor live values
Disconnect power and remove the ice maker to access the tray sensor plug
Measure tray sensor resistance—compare to the chart on the tech sheet at freezer temp
Replace the ice maker if the tray thermistor reads open or above spec
when_to_call_technician: Tray sensor is often integral to the ice maker head—service can swap the assembly if resistance is out of range.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 25
summary: Ice tray sensor resistance too low
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 25 means ice tray sensor shorted. Inspect harness for shorts, replace ice maker thermistor.
meaning: Code 25 is the low-resistance counterpart to code 24—the ice tray thermistor reads below about 660 ohms, indicating a shorted or damaged sensor circuit.
causes: Shorted tray thermistor from water intrusion into the mold body
Pinched harness wire shorting to the freezer liner
Connector bridged by moisture or corrosion
Failed module misreading a good sensor
fixes: Unplug the unit and inspect the ice maker harness along the freezer liner for pinch points
Dry the connector at the ice maker head if frost melt left moisture
Measure tray sensor resistance—values under 660 ohms at freezer temp confirm a short
Install a new ice maker assembly if the sensor is non-replaceable separately
when_to_call_technician: If a new ice maker still shows code 25, the receiver module may need replacement.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 26
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 26 means icemaker motor timeout. Clear ice jam, test motor, replace ice maker gearbox.
meaning: Code 26 means the ice maker drive motor ran longer than allowed without completing its rotation. The gearbox did not return to the start position within the module timeout window.
causes: Frozen ice binding the ejector rake
Stripped plastic gears inside the ice maker gearbox
Failed motor winding drawing low torque
Bent ejector arm hitting the bin auger housing
fixes: Remove the ice bin and melt any ice bridging the ejector with a hair dryer on low
Rotate the ice maker cam by hand—it should move smoothly through 360 degrees
Run a manual harvest from service diagnostics and watch for full rotation
Replace the ice maker head if the motor hums but the cam does not advance
when_to_call_technician: Gearbox replacement is bundled with the ice maker module—service is the fastest path if manual rotation is stiff.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 27
summary: Ice maker motor current too high
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 27 means icemaker motor overcurrent. Clear ice jams, inspect gearbox, replace ice maker motor.
meaning: Code 27 means the ice maker motor is drawing more current than expected—usually from a mechanical bind or failing motor bearings inside the gearbox.
causes: Ice cube stuck under the ejector arm increasing load torque
Worn gearbox bearings creating drag
Motor armature short increasing amp draw
Module incorrectly calibrated for a replaced ice maker
fixes: Empty the bin and clear any cube fragments under the rake
Manually turn the cam—grinding or hard spots indicate gearbox damage
Run one harvest cycle from diagnostics and listen for motor strain
Replace the ice maker assembly if current stays high with a clear mold
when_to_call_technician: Motor overcurrent can damage the module—stop using the ice maker until the gearbox is replaced.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 28
summary: Water inlet solenoid current too high
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 28 means fill solenoid overcurrent. Thaw fill tube, test valve coil, replace solenoid.
meaning: Code 28 means the ice maker water inlet solenoid is drawing excessive current when energized. The module protects the driver circuit by logging code 28 and skipping fill.
causes: Frozen fill tube causing back-pressure on the valve
Shorted solenoid coil on the ice maker fill valve
Mineral buildup restricting the valve pintle
Module driver feeding voltage into a shorted harness
fixes: Inspect the fill tube above the ice maker for an ice plug—thaw with a warm towel
Disconnect power and measure fill valve coil resistance per the tech sheet
Manually trigger a fill from diagnostics and watch for normal water stream into the mold
Replace the ice maker fill valve if coil resistance is below spec
when_to_call_technician: If the valve tests good but code 28 persists, the ice maker module driver needs service replacement.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 29
summary: Water inlet solenoid current too low
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 29 means fill solenoid undercurrent. Check valve wiring, test coil ohms, verify water supply.
meaning: Code 29 means the fill solenoid drew less current than expected—often an open coil, poor connection, or no water reaching the valve when fill was commanded.
causes: Open-circuit solenoid coil on the ice maker valve
Loose wire at the valve connector behind the freezer liner
House water supply off to the refrigerator saddle valve
Kinked quarter-inch water line starving the valve
fixes: Confirm the refrigerator water supply valve under the sink is fully open
Inspect the copper or plastic supply line for kinks behind the fridge
Measure valve coil resistance—an open reading means replace the valve
Run a manual fill from diagnostics and watch for water entering the mold cup
when_to_call_technician: Schedule service if water flows at the disconnected line but code 29 appears during fill—the module or harness may be open.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 30
summary: Ice mold heater not reaching temperature
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 30 means mold heater error. Test heater resistance, check mold thermostat, run harvest test.
meaning: Code 30 means the ice mold heater failed to heat the mold to release temperature during harvest. The module timed out waiting for the thermistor to confirm the mold warmed enough to eject cubes.
causes: Open mold heater element inside the ice maker body
Burned heater connection at the ice maker harness
Failed mold thermostat opening too early
Heavy scale insulating the mold from the heater
fixes: Remove the ice maker and inspect heater wires for heat discoloration
Measure mold heater resistance—it should not read open
Run Service Test 58 heater activation and watch for mold surface warming
Replace the ice maker if the heater is open or does not warm during test
when_to_call_technician: Mold heater replacement is part of the ice maker head—service can swap the assembly if harvest repeatedly fails with code 30.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 31
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 31 means harvest motor range error. Inspect gearbox, test motor current, replace ice maker.
meaning: Code 31 means the harvest motor current or speed fell outside the calibrated range during the eject stroke. The module detected abnormal motor performance compared to factory limits.
causes: Partial ice jam allowing only half rotation of the cam
Worn motor brushes or gearbox slip
Incorrect ice maker installed for the module firmware
Loose motor connector intermittent during harvest
fixes: Clear all ice from the mold and ejector path before retesting
Reseat the motor plug at the ice maker head
Run a forced harvest from diagnostics and count full cam rotation
Replace the ice maker if the motor stutters or stops mid-harvest
when_to_call_technician: Module firmware mismatches require a matched ice maker part number—verify against the tech sheet before ordering.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 32
summary: Harvest thermistor out of calibrated range
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 32 means harvest thermistor range fault. Test mold sensor, check wiring, replace ice maker.
meaning: Code 32 means the harvest thermistor reading during the eject phase was outside the expected resistance band. The module uses this sensor to confirm the mold released ice before returning to freeze.
causes: Drifted harvest thermistor reading high or low vs calibration
Wire fracture in the thermistor harness at the door hinge
Ice shield blocking the sensor from the mold surface
Module EEPROM calibration lost after a power surge
fixes: Run Service Test 58 and record thermistor values during freeze and harvest
Inspect the harness where it exits the freezer door for wire chafing
Compare live readings to the resistance table on the tech sheet
Replace the ice maker if the harvest thermistor is out of spec at known temperatures
when_to_call_technician: If the sensor tests good in isolation, the receiver module may need recalibration or replacement.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 33
summary: Fill tube heater out of range
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 33 means fill tube heater fault. Thaw fill tube, test heater ohms, check ice box fan airflow.
meaning: Code 33 means the fill tube heater resistance or current is outside the acceptable range. A cold fill tube freezes incoming water and prevents the mold from filling—code 33 catches heater circuit failures early.
causes: Open fill tube heater wire along the freezer ceiling
Ice plug in the fill tube from a failed heater
Failed heater element on the plastic fill tube assembly
Ice box fan not running, allowing tube freeze even with a good heater
fixes: Thaw the fill tube with a warm cloth until water can pass freely
Run Service Test 36 to confirm the ice box fan operates
Measure fill tube heater resistance at the connector per tech sheet
Replace the fill tube heater kit if resistance reads open
when_to_call_technician: Fill tube and heater replacement in the freezer ceiling is tedious—service can install the correct tube kit for your model.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 34
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 34 means mold heater range error. Test heater amps, inspect module connection, replace ice maker.
meaning: Code 34 means the mold heater current during harvest was higher or lower than the module expects. Unlike code 30's timeout, code 34 flags marginal heater performance still within time but outside electrical limits.
causes: Partially shorted mold heater drawing excess current
High-resistance connection dropping voltage to the heater
Module current sense resistor drift after years of heat cycles
Wrong voltage ice maker installed on a 120V module
fixes: Inspect heater spade terminals for arcing or carbon buildup
Measure heater resistance and compare to the new-part spec
Run a harvest test and note whether cubes release partially
Replace the ice maker if heater resistance is out of the published range
when_to_call_technician: Current sense faults on the module board require a technician with the model-specific diagnostic software.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 35
summary: Harvest motor speed out of range
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 35 means harvest motor range fault. Clear jams, verify cam timing, replace ice maker gearbox.
meaning: Code 35 means the harvest motor speed profile did not match calibrated limits—too fast or too slow through the eject stroke. The module uses timing feedback to detect slipping gears or weak motors.
causes: Gearbox grease hardened slowing cam rotation
Motor bearing wear causing uneven speed
Ice fragment briefly binding then releasing the rake
Module software version mismatch with the ice maker revision
fixes: Remove the bin and ensure the ejector path is completely clear
Time one manual harvest rotation—it should complete in the seconds listed on the tech sheet
Listen for uneven motor speed during diagnostics harvest
Replace the ice maker if rotation is visibly slow or jerky
when_to_call_technician: If a new ice maker still triggers code 35, update or replace the receiver module per Kenmore bulletin for your serial range.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 36
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 36 means harvest thermistor range fault. Run Test 58, compare mold temps, replace ice maker.
meaning: Code 36 means the harvest thermistor failed a range check distinct from code 32—typically comparing mold temperature delta before and after the heater cycle. The module concludes the sensor cannot reliably report release temperature.
causes: Harvest thermistor decal detached from the mold wall
Sensor reading stuck high after a failed harvest
Water film on the thermistor from a leaking fill valve
End-of-life ice maker with drifted sensor curve
fixes: Run Service Test 58 and record thermistor values at freeze and post-heat
Dry the mold interior and confirm the sensor pad contacts the metal wall
Compare readings to the chart—flat readings across temperature change mean a bad sensor
Replace the ice maker head when the harvest thermistor fails the delta test
when_to_call_technician: Module recalibration after ice maker replacement is model-specific—service can confirm code 36 clears in Test 56.

title: Kenmore Oven F03 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore oven F03 RTD sensor open. Check probe connector behind range, measure resistance at room temp.
meaning: F03 on Kenmore 790-series ranges means the oven temperature sensor (RTD) circuit is open. The electronic range control cannot regulate bake or broil without a valid probe signal.
causes: Sensor plug loose behind the rear panel
Broken sensor wire inside the oven cavity routing
Failed RTD probe internally open
Connector corrosion from steam at the broil element
fixes: Shut off power at the breaker
Remove the rear lower panel and reseat the sensor harness
Measure probe resistance—about 1080 ohms at room temperature on many models
Replace the probe if resistance reads infinite
when_to_call_technician: New probe with correct ohms but persistent F03 indicates ERC wiring service.

title: Kenmore Oven F04 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F04 shorted oven sensor. Inspect probe for bare wire touching cavity, replace RTD if shorted.
meaning: F04 means the oven temperature sensor reads a short circuit. The ERC disables heat to prevent uncontrolled temperature rise.
causes: Sensor wire pinched against the oven liner
Moisture shorting the connector after steam cleaning
Failed probe with internal short
Metal braid touching terminal screws
fixes: Disconnect power and inspect the sensor wire route through the cavity
Dry the connector thoroughly if wet
Measure resistance—near zero ohms indicates a shorted probe
Install a new probe matched to your Kenmore model number
when_to_call_technician: F04 with a disconnected probe means a harness short—trace wiring to the ERC.

title: Kenmore Oven F05 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F05 door lock error on self-clean models. Check latch motor, wiring, and door alignment.
meaning: F05 appears on self-cleaning Kenmore ovens when the door latch cannot lock or unlock on command. Clean and convection cycles may be blocked until the latch reports correct position.
causes: Food debris on the latch hook preventing engagement
Failed latch motor actuator
Warped door hinge misaligning the strike
Broken wire in the door switch harness
fixes: Cancel any active clean cycle and allow the oven to cool
Inspect the latch slot on the door frame for grease buildup
Listen for latch motor movement when selecting self-clean
Verify the door closes flush against the gasket
when_to_call_technician: Latch motor with no movement after cleaning needs model-specific latch assembly replacement.

title: Kenmore Oven F06 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F06 door switch error. Test latch switches, verify lock engages before clean cycle.
meaning: F06 means the door lock switch circuit failed verification. The ERC detected an unexpected open or short in the lock position feedback.
causes: Latch switch stuck from carbon after clean cycles
Switch harness unplugged during prior service
Failed dual-switch latch assembly
ERC not reading switch bounce correctly
fixes: Power off and manually move the latch to feel for binding
Inspect the two microswitches on the latch motor
Reseat the latch harness at the ERC
Run a short bake at 350°F before attempting self-clean again
when_to_call_technician: Replace the door latch assembly when switches test bad or latch motor stalls.

title: Kenmore Oven F07 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F07 lock motor fault. No latch movement on self-clean—replace door lock motor assembly.
meaning: F07 specifically targets the lock motor output circuit. The ERC commanded latch movement but did not detect motor response.
causes: Burned lock motor windings
Relay on ERC not sending voltage to motor
Mechanical jam in latch linkage
Broken motor gear inside the latch
fixes: Disconnect power before inspecting the latch motor
Check for 120 VAC at the motor during lock command in service mode
Lubricate only per service manual—excess grease attracts dust
Replace latch assembly if motor is open or seized
when_to_call_technician: Voltage at motor with no shaft rotation confirms latch motor replacement.

title: Kenmore Oven F08 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F08 cooling fan error. Fan should run during bake—check harness and fan motor.
meaning: F08 means the oven cooling fan did not start when required. Electronic controls and door components depend on airflow during heat operation on Kenmore wall ovens.
causes: Fan blade obstructed by wiring tie
Open fan motor winding
Loose connector at the rear cooling fan
ERC fan relay failed
fixes: Shut off power and spin the cooling fan blade by hand
Remove the rear panel and reseat the fan harness
Listen for fan start within a minute of turning oven on
Clear any foil or storage against rear vents
when_to_call_technician: Fan motor that won't run with good voltage needs cooling fan replacement.

title: Kenmore Oven F09 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F09 over-temperature fault. Shut off breaker, test sensor and ERC—do not use until cleared.
