meaning: F09 is a safety over-temperature fault. The ERC detected oven temperature above the safe limit—often from a welded relay or failed sensor reading low.
causes: Bake relay stuck closed on the ERC
Sensor detached giving false cold reading
Runaway clean cycle with latch fault
External heat source too close to the range
fixes: Turn off the breaker immediately and leave the door closed until cool
Inspect the sensor mounting in the upper oven cavity
Do not restart until F09 cause is identified
Test bake element for continuity to ground when power is off
when_to_call_technician: F09 after cooldown with a good sensor strongly suggests ERC relay failure—replace the control board.

title: Kenmore Oven F10 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F10 temperature runaway. Power off, test oven sensor and ERC relays before reuse.
meaning: F10 indicates the oven heated without proportional control—similar to F09 on Kenmore 790 platforms. The ERC shuts down all heat outputs.
causes: Shorted bake element leaking to ground
ERC bake relay welded shut
Sensor reading far below actual cavity temp
Broil element on continuously from relay fault
fixes: Disconnect power at the breaker for 30 minutes
Measure bake element resistance and ground leakage
Verify the sensor reads rising resistance as the cavity cools
Replace sensor first if readings are erratic
when_to_call_technician: Good sensor and element with immediate F10 on preheat needs ERC replacement.

title: Kenmore Oven F11 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F11 shorted key fault on oven UI. Dry the panel, replace touchpad if short persists.
meaning: F11 means the oven user interface detected a short on the keypad matrix. Buttons may be unresponsive or trigger randomly.
causes: Liquid spilled on the control panel
Steam from cooktop shorting the membrane
Cracked overlay bridging keys
Failed ERC keypad input driver
fixes: Power off and dry the control area thoroughly
Avoid cleaning with excessive water on the touch panel
Unplug ribbon harness at ERC and inspect for corrosion
Replace touchpad if F11 shows with harness disconnected from UI
when_to_call_technician: Touchpad or ERC replacement depending on whether short clears with UI unplugged.

title: Kenmore Oven F12 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F12 stuck oven key. Clean panel, power-cycle—replace UI if a key reads pressed constantly.
meaning: F12 is a stuck-key fault distinct from F11 short. One button appears continuously pressed, blocking other inputs on the Kenmore range console.
causes: Grease under the Start or Cancel pad
Membrane collapse from age
Moisture under the overlay from boil-over
ERC interpreting noise as a held key
fixes: Clean around all buttons with a damp cloth then dry
Press each key several times to free a stuck pad
Power-cycle at the breaker for two minutes
Avoid using foil on racks touching the door sensor
when_to_call_technician: Replace the oven touchpad when F12 appears with a clean dry panel untouched.

title: Kenmore Oven F13 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F13 EEPROM error on oven control. Unplug five minutes, replace ERC if code returns every start.
meaning: F13 means the electronic range control failed a memory checksum at power-up. Stored calibration and timing tables may be corrupt.
causes: Power surge on the range circuit
Moisture on ERC from steam or boil-over
Age-related EEPROM failure
Loose logic supply connector
fixes: Shut off the breaker for five minutes
Inspect ERC area for steam damage behind the console
Reseat all harness plugs into the ERC
Run a short 350°F bake test after reset
when_to_call_technician: Immediate F13 on every power-up requires ERC board replacement matched to model suffix.

title: Kenmore Oven F14 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F14 control memory data fault. Inspect harness, replace electronic range control.
meaning: F14 is a secondary EEPROM data fault on the Kenmore ERC platform. Cooking functions may be unpredictable until the board is replaced.
causes: Brownout during self-clean
Incorrect board substituted in prior repair
Logic ground loose at ERC mount
Manufacturing defect in memory chip batch
fixes: Verify the installed ERC part number against the tech sheet
Tighten ERC mounting screws for good ground
Avoid running self-clean until fault clears
Document serial number for Sears PartsDirect ordering
when_to_call_technician: Persistent F14 after one reset requires a new ERC from Kenmore parts.

title: Kenmore Oven F15 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore F15 oven control comm error. Reseat UI ribbon to ERC, replace board if fault persists.
meaning: F15 indicates internal communication failure within the ERC or between ERC and the touch UI. Display may freeze or show garbage characters.
causes: Loose ribbon cable between touchpad and ERC
Failed ERC microcontroller
Static discharge damaging comm lines
UI and ERC revision mismatch after repair
fixes: Power off and reseat the UI ribbon at both ends
Inspect for burnt pins on the ERC header
Match replacement boards to the exact model revision
Test bake operation after reseat
when_to_call_technician: F15 with verified harness needs ERC or touchpad replacement as a matched set.

title: Kenmore Oven F16 Error Code
summary: Communication error between ERC and UI
meta_description: Kenmore F16 UI-to-ERC link fault. Check ribbon cable, replace touch panel or control board.
meaning: F16 specifically flags lost serial communication between the display UI and the ERC—common after replacing only one of the two assemblies on Kenmore ranges.
causes: Partial board replacement with incompatible firmware
Cut ribbon cable during console removal
Corrosion on low-voltage comm pins
Failed ERC UART output driver
fixes: Compare part numbers on UI and ERC before mixing boards
Replace the ribbon if creased or torn
Run a keypad test through service mode if available
Power-cycle after any harness work
when_to_call_technician: Order UI and ERC together using model and serial when F16 follows a single-board swap.

title: Kenmore Oven F17 Error Code
summary: Probe or meat thermometer circuit fault
meta_description: Kenmore F17 temperature probe jack error. Check built-in probe connection, jack wiring to ERC.
meaning: F17 appears on Kenmore models with a temperature probe jack. The ERC detected an open or short in the external meat probe circuit during convection roast or probe bake.
causes: Probe not fully seated in the jack
Damaged probe cable from kinking
Jack wiring loose at the ERC
Probe tip damaged from high heat exposure
fixes: Insert the probe firmly until it clicks
Test with a known-good Kenmore probe if available
Inspect the jack behind the console for loose wires
Cancel probe mode and use timed bake as a workaround
when_to_call_technician: Good probe with F17 at the jack needs receptacle or ERC input circuit repair.

title: GE Washing Machine E000 Error Code
summary: No active faults in diagnostic memory
meta_description: GE E000 means no errors stored. If washer still fails, run service mode and check latest code.
meaning: E000 corresponds to GE's E00 state—diagnostics found no active fault codes in memory. If the washer misbehaves, an intermittent issue may have cleared or the failure is outside the self-test list.
causes: Prior fault cleared after power-cycle
Intermittent harness now seated
User viewing diagnostic history after repair
Different fault not yet logged twice
fixes: Run a full wash cycle to confirm normal operation
Enter service mode to read the most recent stored code
Reseat fill, drain, and door harnesses preventively
Note any new code that appears twice in a row
when_to_call_technician: E000 displayed but washer still won't run—capture the live code during the failure.

title: GE Washing Machine E001 Error Code
summary: Main control communication or memory fault
meta_description: GE E001 control fault. Unplug five minutes, reseat MCU harnesses.
meaning: E001 indicates the main control or motor control unit failed communication or memory check—similar to GE CCU faults on 24-inch front-load platforms.
causes: Surge on laundry circuit
Loose MCU harness
Moisture on control from inlet leak
Failed control board processor
fixes: Unplug five minutes
Remove top access and reseat MCU connectors
Inspect for burn marks near line input
Run a short rinse after reset
when_to_call_technician: Immediate E001 every start needs MCU or main control replacement.

title: GE Washing Machine E002 Error Code
meta_description: GE E002 dE door lock error. Remove fabric from gasket, listen for latch click.
meaning: E002 maps to GE's door lock fault—the machine will not fill or spin until the door lock confirms closed.
causes: Clothing in door gasket
Broken door hook
Failed lock motor
Door harness wire broken
fixes: Clear gasket fold and press door until click
Inspect strike alignment
Power-cycle two minutes
Listen for buzz without lock engagement
when_to_call_technician: Door lock assembly when door closes flush but E002 persists.

title: GE Washing Machine E003 Error Code
meta_description: GE E003 3E2 motor overcurrent. Reduce load, unplug 15 min, check for tub obstruction.
meaning: E003 means excessive current on the drive motor—often overload, jam, or failing motor inverter.
causes: Overloaded drum
Foreign object in tub
Failing motor windings
MCU not regulating current
fixes: Remove heavy items
Spin drum by hand when empty
Unplug 15 minutes to cool inverter
Retry small load
when_to_call_technician: E003 on light loads after reset needs motor or MCU service.

title: GE Washing Machine E004 Error Code
meta_description: GE E004 4E valve fault. Open supply, clean screens, test valves during fill.
meaning: E004 indicates the inlet valve circuit failed—valves not opening, stuck open, or not reporting correctly.
causes: Closed supply valves
Clogged inlet screens
Stuck-open solenoid
Failed valve coil
fixes: Open hot and cold valves fully
Rinse inlet screens into bucket
Watch for fill through door glass
Shut off water if tub fills while unplugged
when_to_call_technician: Strong flow at hoses with no fill needs inlet valve WH13X26534 or equivalent.

title: GE Washing Machine E005 Error Code
summary: Drain pump problem or long drain
meta_description: GE E005 5E drain fault. Clean pump filter, straighten drain hose, test pump.
meaning: E005 means drain timed out—the pump ran but water remained, matching GE 5E on front-load washers.
causes: Clogged pump filter
Kinked drain hose
Failed pump motor
Excess suds slowing drain
fixes: Open lower access and clean pump filter into a pan
Straighten drain hose with air gap at standpipe
Run spin drain and listen for pump
Use HE detergent only
when_to_call_technician: Clear filter with E005 recurring needs drain pump replacement.

title: GE Washing Machine E006 Error Code
meta_description: GE E006 E42 motor speed fault. Check beltless drum spin, reduce load, service MCU.
meaning: E006 corresponds to motor speed faults—drum commanded to spin but RPM feedback never reached target.
causes: Overloaded or unbalanced load
Motor tach failure
MCU drive fault
Mechanical drag in rear bearing
fixes: Redistribute laundry evenly
Remove excess items
Spin drum by hand
Unplug 15 minutes and retry
when_to_call_technician: E006 on small balanced loads needs motor or MCU diagnosis.

title: GE Washing Machine E007 Error Code
summary: Pressure sensor or water level fault
meta_description: GE E007 1E pressure error. Check air dome hose from tub to control.
meaning: E007 means the pressure switch or sensor did not report a valid water level during fill or drain.
causes: Pressure hose disconnected
Hose pinched or leaking air
Dome port clogged
Failed pressure sensor on control
fixes: Trace clear hose from tub to control
Blow through hose to confirm open path
Reinstall without kinks
Run fill cycle and observe level
when_to_call_technician: Good hose with E007 every fill needs pressure switch or control.

title: GE Washing Machine E008 Error Code
summary: Heater relay error on main board
meta_description: GE E008 HE heater relay fault. May need new main PCB—verify water fills before heat step.
meaning: E008 indicates the heater relay on the main control failed—internal heat models cannot warm water.
causes: Relay welded on PCB
Open heating element
Water level too low during heat
Thermal cutout tripped
fixes: Confirm tub fills normally
Avoid heat cycles until diagnosed
Inspect heater terminals when powered off
Power-cycle five minutes once
when_to_call_technician: E008 with good element and fill needs main control WH22X36456 or model PCB.

title: GE Washing Machine E009 Error Code
meta_description: GE E009 9E1 low voltage. Test outlet 108–132 VAC, tighten cord terminals.
meaning: E009 means line voltage dropped below the safe minimum—often shared circuits or loose connections.
causes: Loose dryer cord sharing neutral
Undersized extension cord
Weak breaker contact
Utility brownout
fixes: Test voltage at terminal block under load
Tighten cord screws
Dedicated 20A laundry circuit
Reset breaker fully
when_to_call_technician: Low voltage at washer with good outlet needs electrician.

title: GE Washing Machine E010 Error Code
meta_description: GE E010 9E2 high voltage. Test for voltage above 132 VAC—contact utility if high.
meaning: E010 indicates supply voltage exceeded the upper safe limit.
causes: Lost neutral causing high leg voltage
Incorrect utility transformer tap
Shared neutral with large loads
Surge without proper ground
fixes: Measure voltage at outlet and terminal
Shut off if reading above 132 VAC
Contact utility for high leg
Verify three-wire grounded outlet
when_to_call_technician: House wiring or utility issue when voltage consistently high.

title: GE Washing Machine E011 Error Code
meta_description: GE E011 tE thermistor error. Reseat NTC at tub, measure resistance at room temp.
meaning: E011 maps to GE tE—temperature sensor out of range on heat or monitoring circuits.
causes: Thermistor unplugged
Scale on sensor bulb
Open sensor wire
Control input failure
fixes: Reseat two-wire NTC at heater or tub
Compare ohms to GE chart at 77°F
Run warm cycle after reseat
Descale if hard water present
when_to_call_technician: Correct ohms with E011 needs thermistor or control.

title: GE Washing Machine E012 Error Code
summary: Leakage detected in tub or base
meta_description: GE E012 LE leak error. Inspect door boot, dispenser, and pump hoses for moisture.
meaning: E012 is GE's leakage fault—moisture detected where it should not be, often at the door boot or sump.
causes: Torn door boot
Loose tub-to-pump hose clamp
Dispenser overfill leak
Drain pump seal seep
fixes: Inspect gray boot for holes or debris in fold
Check clamps at pump and tub outlet
Run small cycle and watch for drips with power off after
Dry base pan if equipped with aqua sensor
when_to_call_technician: Active leak with E012 needs boot or hose service before reuse.

title: GE Washing Machine E013 Error Code
meta_description: GE E013 OE overflow. Shut off water, check inlet valves and pressure hose immediately.
meaning: E013 means the water level exceeded maximum—pressure switch saw overfill or inlet valve stuck open.
causes: Inlet valve stuck open
Pressure hose disconnected
Prior drain fault leaving water
Control fill relay welded
fixes: Turn off hot and cold supply now
Unplug and run drain if safe
Inspect pressure tube to control
Do not wash until tub verified empty and valves shut
when_to_call_technician: Water entering while unplugged requires inlet valve replacement urgently.

title: GE Washing Machine E014 Error Code
meta_description: GE E014 UE unbalance. Redistribute clothes, level washer, avoid single heavy items.
meaning: E014 is GE's unbalanced load fault—spin aborted when weight distribution prevented safe RPM.
causes: Single heavy item alone
Clothes clumped on one side
Washer not level
Worn shocks on older units
fixes: Pause and spread items around drum
Add towels with one heavy piece
Level feet and lock nuts
Retry spin only at lower speed first
when_to_call_technician: UE on full balanced loads after leveling needs suspension service.

title: GE Dryer E001 Error Code
summary: Main control EEPROM or memory fault
meta_description: GE dryer E001 control memory error. Unplug five minutes, replace board if persistent.
meaning: E001 indicates the dryer main control failed memory verification at startup.
causes: Power surge
Loose harness
Moisture on board
EEPROM failure
fixes: Unplug five minutes
Reseat main harness
Inspect for burn marks
Test timed dry
when_to_call_technician: Immediate E001 every start needs electronic control.

title: GE Dryer E002 Error Code
meta_description: GE E002 door switch fault. Close door firmly, replace switch if no drum rotation.
meaning: E002 means the door switch never reported closed.
causes: Worn catch
Broken switch
Hinge sag
Failed switch
fixes: Close until light out
Inspect plunger
Replace switch
Tighten hinges
when_to_call_technician: Door switch is owner-serviceable on many GE models.

title: GE Dryer E003 Error Code
meta_description: GE dryer E003 is an open exhaust thermistor fault. Reseat the sensor plug at the blower housing, inspect wiring, and replace the thermistor if readings stay open.
meaning: E003 indicates open exhaust temperature sensor circuit.
causes: Loose plug
Broken wire
Open sensor
Lint on connector
fixes: Reseat thermistor
Vacuum connector area
Measure ohms at room temp
Run low heat test
when_to_call_technician: Open sensor with good wiring needs replacement.

title: GE Dryer E004 Error Code
meta_description: GE E004 moisture bar fault. Clean strips inside drum with alcohol.
meaning: E004 means auto-dry moisture bars read open.
causes: Unplugged harness
Softener coating
Corrosion
Open wire
fixes: Clean moisture bars
Check bulkhead harness
Run sensor dry test
Inspect door harness
when_to_call_technician: Persistent E004 with clean bars needs harness.

title: GE Dryer E005 Error Code
meta_description: GE E005 thermal fault. Clean vent first, test fuse on blower housing.
meaning: E005 indicates thermal protection opened—usually from restricted venting.
causes: Crushed vent
Lint duct clog
Blocked cap
Cycling thermostat stuck
fixes: Clean full vent path
Test thermal fuse continuity
Replace fuse after fixing airflow
Feel strong exhaust at cap
when_to_call_technician: Fuse blows again immediately—deep vent service needed.

title: GE Dryer E006 Error Code
summary: Communication error between UI and control
meta_description: GE dryer E006 is a console-to-board communication fault. Reseat the ribbon cable at the main control, check for corrosion, and replace the UI if E006 returns.
meaning: E006 means user interface lost communication with main control.
causes: Loose ribbon
Surge damage
Corrosion
Board mismatch
fixes: Reseat UI harness
Inspect ribbon
Power-cycle
Match revisions on replace
when_to_call_technician: E006 after reseat needs UI board.

title: GE Dryer E007 Error Code
meta_description: GE dryer E007 means a shorted moisture sensor bar circuit. Dry the sensor bars, inspect the harness at the drum, and replace the sensor if the short persists.
meaning: E007 is shorted moisture sensor circuit.
causes: Wet harness
Debris bridging bars
Pinched wire
Failed strip
fixes: Dry interior bars
Test with harness unplugged
Run timed dry
Replace sensor if shorted
when_to_call_technician: Sensor replacement when short persists.

title: GE Dryer E008 Error Code
summary: Gas valve or igniter circuit fault
meta_description: GE E008 gas heat fault. On gas models—check igniter glow and valve coils.
meaning: E008 on gas GE dryers indicates heat circuit failure—igniter, valve coils, or flame sensor.
causes: Broken igniter
Weak valve coil
Flame sensor dirty
No gas supply
fixes: Verify gas valve open at pipe
Watch igniter glow through peephole
Clean flame sensor rod
Listen for valve click
when_to_call_technician: No glow or weak glow needs igniter WE04X24550 or valve coils.

title: GE Dryer E009 Error Code
meta_description: GE dryer E009 indicates a shorted keypad on the console. Dry the panel after spills, clean around buttons, and replace the UI assembly if E009 latches.
meaning: E009 means stuck or shorted user interface key.
causes: Spill on panel
Shorted membrane
Static
Failed UI
fixes: Dry panel hours
Power off
Replace UI if persists
Avoid wet cleaning
when_to_call_technician: UI replacement for persistent E009.

title: GE Dryer E010 Error Code
meta_description: GE E010 vent restriction. Clean lint screen, hose, and exterior cap—fire safety priority.
meaning: E010 indicates severe exhaust restriction detected—similar to vent safety codes on modern GE dryers.
causes: Lint screen packed
Long crushed hose
Wall duct clog
Roof cap blocked
fixes: Wash lint screen
Vacuum hose both ends
Clean exterior cap
Run empty timed cycle test
when_to_call_technician: Professional duct cleaning if weak airflow persists.

title: GE Dryer E011 Error Code
meta_description: GE E011 static fault. Ground yourself in dry winter laundry rooms.
meaning: E011 is static on the touch UI in low humidity.
causes: Dry air
Synthetic loads
Ungrounded outlet
Sensitive UI
fixes: Touch ground before panel
Unplug one minute
Add humidity
Verify grounded outlet
when_to_call_technician: Every-start E011 with good ground needs UI.

title: GE Dryer E012 Error Code
summary: Drive motor or belt switch fault

causes: Broken belt
Belt switch open
Failed motor
Door switch cascade
fixes: Power off and inspect belt through front panel on top-load styles
Verify belt switch depressed by idler
Spin drum by hand
Listen for motor hum
when_to_call_technician: Motor or belt switch replacement when drum won't turn.

title: GE Dryer E013 Error Code

Surge
Age
Wrong board

Reseat harness
Verify board part
Short air test

title: GE Dryer E014 Error Code

Loose logic supply
Wrong variant

Reseat connectors
Match part numbers
Document serial

Factory error
Aftermarket board
Incomplete pairing

Install both boards
Power-cycle
Avoid mixing revs

title: GE Dishwasher C1 Error Code

Kinked drain hose
Blocked air gap
Disposal plug not removed
fixes: Clean sump filter
Verify high loop or air gap
Run disposal
Press Start/Reset after clearing
when_to_call_technician: C1 with clear path and humming pump needs drain pump.

title: GE Dishwasher C2 Error Code
meta_description: GE C2 severe drain fault. Press RESET, clear full drain path, check pump.
meaning: C2 indicates total pump-out exceeded seven minutes—more severe than C1.
causes: Partially blocked drain line
Failed pump impeller
Check valve stuck
Kinked hose under sink
fixes: Press RESET to stop beep
Clean filter and air gap thoroughly
Disconnect hose to bucket test
Inspect check valve
when_to_call_technician: Standing water after C2 with clear plumbing needs pump motor.

title: GE Dishwasher C3 Error Code
meta_description: GE C3 no drain. Breaker off 30 seconds, reset, service if water remains.
meaning: C3 means the dishwasher will not drain after reset sequence per GE support.
causes: Pump motor failed
Heavy debris in sump
Control not sending voltage to pump
Jammed impeller

Remove standing water with cup
Clean sump and spin impeller by hand
Retry cancel-drain
when_to_call_technician: C3 with clear sump and silent pump needs drain pump WD26X10013 or equivalent.

title: GE Dishwasher C4 Error Code

meta_description: GE C4 float fault. Check float in tub base, clear drain, inspect inlet valve.
meaning: C4 appears when machine filled twice after power failure or float switch stuck—GE official definition.
causes: Float stuck up
Inlet valve leaking after outage
Clogged drain leaving water
Suds lifting float

Dry tub and verify float moves down
Inspect inlet valve for drip
Clear drain path
when_to_call_technician: Water enters tub when off—replace inlet valve before reuse.

title: GE Dishwasher C5 Error Code
meta_description: GE C5 abnormal short drain. May indicate sensor or control timing fault.
meaning: C5 means pump-out completed faster than expected—often pressure or float sensor anomaly.
causes: Pressure sensor false empty
Loose float sensor
Control timing corruption
Air gap siphon

Reseat float and pressure switches
Power-cycle breaker 30 seconds
Run full normal cycle test
when_to_call_technician: C5 with water still present needs sensor or control diagnosis.

title: GE Dishwasher C6 Error Code

Long dead-leg plumbing
Cold tap selected at sink
Failed dishwasher heater

Set water heater to 120–140°F
Select high-temp wash option
Verify heat during wash if model has heater
when_to_call_technician: C6 with steaming tap at start needs heater element test.

title: GE Dishwasher C7 Error Code

Compare ohms to chart

Measure element
Replace erratic sensor
Inspect broil relay

meaning: F14 is secondary EEPROM corruption.

Bad ground
Chip defect

Tighten screws
Avoid clean until fixed
Document serial

Test bake

meaning: F16 means lost serial link between touch UI and ERC.

title: GE Oven F17 Error Code
meta_description: GE oven F17 indicates a meat probe jack fault. Seat the probe fully, inspect jack wiring at the ERC, and replace the probe or receptacle if F17 persists while inserted.
meaning: F17 appears when meat probe circuit is open or shorted on probe-equipped GE ovens.
causes: Probe not seated
Damaged cable
Loose jack wires
Burnt tip
fixes: Insert firmly
Test known-good probe
Inspect jack
Use timed bake workaround
when_to_call_technician: Good probe with F17—jack or ERC.

title: LG Dryer tE1 Error Code
summary: Inlet thermistor reading out of range
meta_description: LG dryer tE1 means the inlet air thermistor failed. Clean the heat exchanger area, reseat the sensor plug, and test after vent service.
meaning: tE1 is a thermistor sub-fault on LG condenser and heat-pump dryers—the sensor at the inlet heat exchanger sent resistance outside the board's valid range. Unlike exhaust tE, tE1 points to the cooler inlet path where lint and condensate collect.
causes: Condensate dripping onto the inlet thermistor harness after a long steam cycle
Lint mat blocking airflow across the inlet sensor housing
Thermistor wire chafed where the drum seal meets the front bulkhead
Failed inlet thermistor after repeated overheat from blocked condenser coils
fixes: Unplug the dryer and vacuum the condenser coil behind the lower front panel per your model guide
Locate the inlet thermistor near the heat exchanger and firmly reseat its two-pin connector
Wipe moisture from the sensor pocket and allow the cabinet to dry one hour before retest
Run a Timed Dry on low heat for ten minutes and confirm tE1 does not return at startup
when_to_call_technician: tE1 after coil cleaning and connector reseat needs thermistor resistance checked against LG spec—open readings require sensor or board service.

title: LG Washing Machine u5 Error Code
summary: Door lock cannot release after cycle
meta_description: LG washer u5 means unable to unlock door. Wait two minutes, drain water, then check door lock motor and wiring if u5 persists.
meaning: u5 reports Unable to Unlock—the door lock motor energized to release but the latch never reported an open position within the timeout. Water may still be in the tub, which is why LG blocks the door until the fault clears or you drain manually.
causes: Standing water in the tub keeping pressure on the door seal
Door lock motor burned out on the release stroke
Broken door latch hook binding the lock mechanism
Control board relay stuck energizing lock without releasing
fixes: Wait two minutes without forcing the door—LG may auto-retry the unlock sequence
Open the lower front access panel, drain the emergency hose into a pan, then press Start/Pause to retry unlock
Unplug for five minutes, restore power, and run Spin Only after confirming the drum is empty
If the door opens manually after drain but u5 returns every cycle, stop forcing the latch and schedule lock service
when_to_call_technician: Door stays locked with an empty drum after power reset—the door lock assembly or main PCB likely needs replacement.

title: LG Washing Machine CL Error Code
summary: Child Lock active—not a mechanical fault
meta_description: LG washer CL means Child Lock is on. Hold Temp and Options three seconds to disable—buttons stay dead until CL clears.
meaning: CL is Child Lock, not a failure code. LG disables cycle selection and temperature buttons so children cannot start or change a wash. The display may flash CL when any locked button is pressed.
causes: Child Lock enabled by holding Temp and Options together during cleaning
SmartThinQ app lock turned on remotely
Power surge restoring lock state on some firmware builds
Guest accidentally activating lock while wiping the control panel
fixes: Press and hold the Temp and Options buttons together for three seconds until CL disappears
On models with a dedicated Lock key, hold Lock three seconds instead
Open the ThinQ app and disable Control Lock under appliance settings
Confirm buttons respond after CL clears by changing spin speed on an idle machine
when_to_call_technician: Only if the panel stays unresponsive after CL is gone—then suspect UI board failure, not the lock feature.

title: LG Dishwasher 6E Error Code
summary: Inlet water flow too low during fill
meta_description: LG dishwasher 6E means weak inlet flow. Open supply valve, clean inlet screen, straighten hose, and confirm hot water at the sink.
meaning: 6E is LG's inlet flow error—the fill valve opened but the flow meter or level switch did not see enough water within the programmed window. The cycle aborts before wash to avoid running the pump dry.
causes: Hot supply valve under the sink closed or only partially open
Inlet hose kinked where it passes through the cabinet side panel
Mesh strainer at the dishwasher inlet clogged with pipe scale
Failed inlet valve solenoid passing only a trickle on one side
fixes: Run hot water at the kitchen sink for thirty seconds, then open the dishwasher supply valve fully
Inspect the copper or braided inlet line for sharp bends behind the kick plate
Disconnect the inlet hose at the dishwasher, rinse the internal screen into a bucket, and reconnect
Power-cycle two minutes and start a Normal cycle—listen for steady fill hiss in the first minute
when_to_call_technician: Strong flow at the disconnected inlet with 6E during fill points to inlet valve or flow sensor replacement under the tub.

title: LG Refrigerator CHE Error Code
meta_description: LG CHE means compressor system error. Power reset, check rear harness at inverter board—persistent CHE needs compressor service.
meaning: CHE is a Compressor Error on LG linear inverter refrigerators—the main board lost valid feedback from the inverter or compressor windings. Cooling may stop while the display shows CHE, and freezer temps climb within hours.
causes: Loose three-wire harness between main PCB and inverter board at the rear bottom
Inverter board overheated after condenser coils were never cleaned
Compressor winding fault detected by the inverter driver
Power surge damaging inverter communication circuitry
fixes: Unplug or flip the breaker for five minutes for a full board reset
Pull the fridge forward safely and vacuum condenser coils at the bottom rear grille
Reseat the inverter-to-compressor harness plug—LG marks it with a colored clip
Monitor freezer temp for four hours after reset; note whether CHE returns on schedule
when_to_call_technician: CHE that reappears within three hours after coil cleaning and harness reseat requires inverter board or compressor diagnosis—live compressor tests only.

title: LG Refrigerator CLE Error Code
summary: Control Lock enabled on the display
meta_description: LG CLE on fridge display means Control Lock is active. Hold the Lock button three seconds to unlock dispensers and touch keys.
meaning: CLE is Control Lock Engaged—the same function as CL on other LG displays. Water and ice dispensers and temperature keys are disabled until lock is cleared. It is not a cooling or sensor fault.
causes: Lock button held during door or dispenser cleaning
ThinQ app Control Lock toggled on
Factory default lock state after firmware update on some door-in-door models
Child pressed lock combo while leaning on the panel
fixes: Press and hold the Lock button on the dispenser or door UI for three seconds until CLE clears
Check SmartThinQ for an active lock icon on the refrigerator card
On models without a Lock label, hold Water and Ice together three seconds per the door chart
Dispense water once to confirm the panel accepts input after CLE disappears
when_to_call_technician: Call only if dispenser buttons remain dead after CLE clears—UI board failure is separate from lock.

title: Bosch Dishwasher E22 Error Code
summary: Filter blocked or not locked in place
meta_description: Bosch E22 means drain filter issue. Remove, clean, and lock the filter assembly—E22 clears when filter seats correctly.
meaning: E22 on Bosch dishwashers indicates the drain filter is clogged, missing, or not locked—the wash system monitors filter presence and flow before draining.
causes: Filter not twisted fully clockwise into locked position
Food debris packed in the mesh filter
Fine glass grit blocking filter outlet
Filter housing o-ring misaligned after cleaning
fixes: Remove lower rack and twist filter counterclockwise, lift out
Rinse all filter sections under hot running water
Reinstall and turn clockwise until it locks with a click
Run a quick rinse cycle to confirm E22 clears
when_to_call_technician: E22 with clean locked filter may indicate drain pump or flow sensor service.

title: Frigidaire Dishwasher i20 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire i20 no-fill fault. Open supply valve, clean inlet screen, verify float moves freely.
meaning: i20 is Frigidaire's no-water fault—the fill valve opened but insufficient water entered within the programmed time on Gallery and Professional models.
causes: Under-sink supply valve closed
Inlet screen clogged at dishwasher connection
Kinked copper inlet line
Stuck overfill float preventing fill recognition
fixes: Open dishwasher supply valve fully
Disconnect inlet hose and rinse screen into bucket
Straighten inlet line behind kick plate
Tap float dome—it should bob freely
when_to_call_technician: Water at disconnected hose with empty tub needs inlet valve replacement.

title: Kenmore Washer F21 Error Code
summary: Long drain on Whirlpool-built Kenmore HE washers
meta_description: Kenmore F21 long drain—same as Whirlpool. Clean pump filter, fix drain hose air gap, use HE detergent.
meaning: F21 on Kenmore HE front-load washers (built by Whirlpool) means drain time exceeded limits—water remains when spin should start.
causes: Pump filter clogged with lint and debris
Drain hose kinked or too deep in standpipe
Excess suds from non-HE detergent
Failed drain pump
fixes: Unplug and clean front pump filter into a shallow pan
Reinstall hose with air gap at standpipe
Use HE detergent only
Press Pause/Cancel and restart after cleaning
when_to_call_technician: F21 after clean filter and hose check needs pump or internal hose service.

title: GE Washer E2 Error Code
summary: Drain pump unable to empty tub
meta_description: GE washer E2 drain fault on Profile and front-load models. Clean pump filter, check drain hose, clear standpipe.
meaning: E2 on GE washers indicates a drain failure—the pump ran but water level did not drop enough before the next cycle step.
causes: Pump filter or coin trap blocked
Drain hose kinked behind unit
Standpipe clogged or sealed airtight
Drain pump motor failure
fixes: Open access panel and clean pump filter per model diagram
Straighten drain hose maintaining air gap
Confirm standpipe drains freely when pouring water in
Run spin drain after filter service
when_to_call_technician: Pump hums without draining after filter clean needs pump replacement.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F8 E4 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F8 E4 leak pan fault. Dry base, fix fill line leaks, reconnect float—same as Whirlpool platform.
meaning: F8 E4 on Maytag dishwashers (Whirlpool platform) triggers when water hits the base drip tray or the float switch faults—the unit drains and locks out wash.
causes: Loose fill connection on new install
Float switch unplugged
Drain hose drip during pump-out
Over-sudsing into base pan
fixes: Turn off water supply
Press Cancel twice after drying pan and fixing leak
Verify float switch connector clicks locked
Run short cycle and watch toe-kick for new moisture
when_to_call_technician: Dry pan with recurring F8 E4 needs internal hose or tub seal service.

title: Maytag Washer F5 Error Code
summary: Lid lock fault on top-load models
meta_description: Maytag F5 lid lock error. Close lid firmly, inspect strike—F5 E2/E3 are related lock codes on HE models.
meaning: F5 on Maytag top-load washers means the lid lock switch did not confirm closure before agitation or spin—the machine stops for safety.
causes: Lid not fully closed
Broken lid strike
Failed lid lock actuator on MVW series
Loose wire at lid switch harness
fixes: Close lid until click
Inspect strike on lid for bending
Remove items preventing lid from seating
Unplug two minutes and retry
when_to_call_technician: Lid lock replacement if F5 with lid fully closed.

title: Samsung Washer ND Error Code
meta_description: Samsung ND means no drain detected. Clean drain pump filter, check drain hose, verify standpipe is clear.
meaning: ND is Samsung's No Drain fault—the drain pump ran but the water level sensor still detected water after the drain phase. The washer stops before spin to avoid sloshing a full tub.
causes: Drain pump filter clogged with coins, lint, or hair
Drain hose kinked or pushed too far into standpipe
Blocked house drain or shared sink clog
Failed drain pump motor humming without moving water
fixes: Open the small access panel at the front bottom, drain into a pan, twist out the filter
Straighten the drain hose with an air gap at the standpipe
Run the kitchen disposal 30 seconds if sharing a drain line
Retry Spin Only after filter cleaning
when_to_call_technician: Clean filter with pump humming but no water movement needs drain pump replacement.

title: Samsung Washer 5E Error Code
summary: Drain error—same family as ND on some models
meta_description: Samsung 5E drain fault. Clean pump filter, check hose routing, clear suds—5E and ND share drain troubleshooting.
meaning: 5E indicates a drain problem on Samsung washers—many models alternate between 5E and ND for the same slow-drain detection depending on firmware year.
causes: Pump filter obstruction
Excess suds slowing pump flow
Drain hose sealed airtight at standpipe
Partial pump impeller failure
fixes: Clean the drain pump filter completely
Use HE detergent at the marked line only
Ensure drain hose is not taped into standpipe
Power off two minutes and run Drain cycle
when_to_call_technician: 5E after verified clear drain path needs pump or pressure sensor service.

title: Samsung Washer 9C8 Error Code
meta_description: Samsung 9C8 means unstable AC supply. Plug into dedicated outlet, avoid generators—9C8 often follows storms.
meaning: 9C8 reports the control detected abnormal line frequency or voltage—common after brownouts, generator power, or loose neutral connections on the laundry circuit.
causes: Shared circuit with heavy loads causing voltage sag
Extension cord underrated for motor inrush
Generator power with unstable frequency
Loose outlet or neutral at the washer plug
fixes: Plug directly into a grounded 120V outlet rated for the washer
Move off generator or inverter backup power
Have an electrician check outlet voltage under load if lights dim on spin
Power off five minutes after storm before retry
when_to_call_technician: 9C8 on stable utility power with verified 120V needs main control board inspection.

title: Samsung Washer 8C Error Code
summary: Mem sensor or load balance fault
meta_description: Samsung 8C mem sensor error. Redistribute load evenly, level washer, retry spin at lower RPM.
meaning: 8C relates to Samsung's mems sensor or vibration detection—the board measured excessive drum movement or invalid sensor feedback before high-speed spin.
causes: Single heavy item clumped on one side
Washer not level front to back
Worn suspension allowing tub strike
Sensor harness loose after transport
fixes: Redistribute laundry around the drum perimeter
Level feet with a bubble level and lock nuts
Remove one heavy item and wash separately with towels
Run spin at 800 RPM test before full cycle
when_to_call_technician: 8C on balanced full loads after leveling suggests suspension or sensor replacement.

title: Samsung Washer 3C Error Code
meta_description: Samsung 3C motor error. Power reset 15 min, reduce load—3C on light load needs motor service.
meaning: 3C indicates the motor inverter could not drive the drum at commanded speed—overload, jam, or failed motor/inverter components.
causes: Overloaded drum with wet bedding
Object jamming drum rotation
Motor harness unplugged at inverter
Failed stator or rotor on direct-drive models
fixes: Unplug 15 minutes to reset inverter
Remove several items and spin drum by hand
Avoid back-to-back heavy cycles without cooldown
Retry small load before washing comforters again
when_to_call_technician: 3C on empty or light load after reset requires motor or inverter service.

title: Samsung Washer SUD Error Code
meta_description: Samsung SUD suds lockout. Use HE detergent sparingly, run Rinse+Spin, clean drain filter if water remains.
meaning: SUD means the washer detected too much foam—the foam blocks accurate water level reading and Samsung extends rinse time or pauses the cycle until suds drop.
causes: Non-HE detergent or overdose
Soft water needing less detergent than the max line
Drain slow from clogged filter trapping sudsy water
Liquid detergent poured into wrong compartment
fixes: Run Rinse and Spin with no detergent
Switch to HE and use lowest recommended dose
Clean drain pump filter if water stays after SUD
Avoid fabric softener in main wash on HE models
when_to_call_technician: SUD every cycle with correct HE dose and clean filter may indicate pressure sensor fault.

title: Samsung Washer SC Error Code
meta_description: Samsung SC system communication error. Power-cycle breaker five minutes; SC at every start needs PCB service.
meaning: SC indicates a System Communication error between the main PCB and sub-boards or the display—cycles may freeze or cancel mid-program.
causes: Loose ribbon cable after moving washer
Power surge damaging comm chips
Moisture on connectors in humid laundry rooms
Failed main or sub PCB
fixes: Turn breaker off five full minutes
Ensure dedicated grounded outlet without extension cord
Dry control area if condensation present
Note whether SC appears at boot or mid-wash for service
when_to_call_technician: SC every power-on after reset needs board replacement with matching model number.

title: Samsung Washer UR Error Code
meta_description: Samsung UR unbalance fault. Spread clothes evenly, add items to small loads, level the machine.
meaning: UR stops spin when the drum is unbalanced—Samsung measures weight distribution and aborts high RPM rather than walk the machine across the floor.
causes: Heavy items clumped together
Single towel or mat alone in drum
Machine not level
Worn shock absorbers on older units
fixes: Open door and redistribute clothes 360° around drum
Add one or two towels when washing one heavy piece
Adjust front feet until level bubble centers
Use Spin only at medium speed test
when_to_call_technician: UR on full balanced loads after leveling needs suspension service.

title: Samsung Washer NF Error Code
meta_description: Samsung NF no-fill error. Open supply valves, clean inlet screens, check kinked fill hoses.
meaning: NF means No Fill—the valves opened but the water level never rose within the fill timeout on Samsung top and front loaders.
causes: Supply valves closed or partially shut
Clogged inlet mesh screens
Kinked fill hoses
Failed inlet valve solenoid
fixes: Turn both wall valves fully open
Disconnect fill hoses and rinse screens in bucket
Pull washer forward one inch to remove kinks
Watch door glass during fill for incoming water
when_to_call_technician: Strong flow at hoses with empty tub needs inlet valve or level sensor service.

title: Samsung Washer SE Error Code
summary: Motor sensor or hall feedback fault
meta_description: Samsung SE sensor error on motor circuit. Reset power, check load—SE with 3C needs motor service.
meaning: SE indicates a Sensor Error on the motor feedback circuit—the hall or tach signal did not match commanded drum speed.
causes: Motor harness loose at inverter
Hall sensor failure after overload
Control board motor input damaged
Drum mechanically jammed intermittently
fixes: Unplug 15 minutes and retry with light load
Spin drum by hand when off
Avoid overloading with soaked wool
Document if SE appears with 3C together
when_to_call_technician: SE repeating on light load after reset needs motor or inverter diagnosis.

title: Samsung Dishwasher OC Error Code
meta_description: Samsung dishwasher OC overflow fault. Turn off water supply, check inlet valve and float, cancel and drain.
meaning: OC means Overflow Condition—Samsung detected too much water in the tub or base, often from a stuck-open inlet valve or drain failure leaving water between cycles.
causes: Inlet valve stuck open from scale
Drain blocked leaving prior cycle water
Float switch stuck
Oversuds pushing water over the door
fixes: Shut off under-sink supply valve immediately
Cancel cycle and run drain if available
Inspect float in tub corner—it must move freely
Use recommended detergent amount
when_to_call_technician: Tub fills while unplugged—inlet valve must be replaced before use.

title: Samsung Dishwasher PC Error Code
summary: Zone wash or half-load diverter fault
meta_description: Samsung PC error on WaterWall models. Remove obstruction from reflector path, reset power, service diverter if PC persists.
meaning: PC on Samsung WaterWall dishwashers indicates the zone wash motor or reflector did not reach the expected position—the board cannot route high-pressure spray correctly.
causes: Utensil blocking WaterWall reflector travel
Diverter motor stalled from food debris
Loose connector at zone motor
Control not driving diverter
fixes: Remove lower rack and check reflector path is clear
Clean sump area under filter
Power off two minutes at breaker
Retry normal cycle without pots blocking the wall
when_to_call_technician: Clear path with PC every cycle needs zone motor or control service.

title: Samsung Dishwasher 5C Error Code
meta_description: Samsung dishwasher 5C drain fault. Clean filter, check drain hose high loop, verify disposal knockout.
meaning: 5C is Samsung's drain fault for dishwashers—same troubleshooting path as washer 5E/ND but on the dishwasher drain pump and hose routing.
causes: Filter clogged with food paste
Drain hose sagging without high loop
Garbage disposal knockout still in place
Drain pump impeller blocked
fixes: Remove and rinse filter assembly under hot water
Confirm drain hose arcs up 10+ inches before sink connection
Run disposal 30 seconds with water
Use cancel drain on models that support forced drain
when_to_call_technician: Pump hums with clean filter but water stays—drain pump service needed.

title: Samsung Dishwasher 3C Error Code
meta_description: Samsung dishwasher 3C motor error. Power reset, clear sump debris—3C on empty cycle needs motor service.
meaning: 3C on Samsung dishwashers indicates the circulation motor did not start or report speed—the wash pump cannot spray water through the arms.
causes: Glass shard in wash impeller
Motor thermal trip from repeated heavy loads
Failed circulation motor capacitor
Wiring corrosion at motor plug after leak
fixes: Power off 15 minutes to cool motor
Remove filter and inspect sump with flashlight only
Reinstall filter and run short rinse empty
Stop use if 3C returns on empty tub
when_to_call_technician: 3C without debris visible requires wash motor replacement.

title: Samsung Dryer dC Error Code
summary: Door open during tumble or heat
meta_description: Samsung dryer dC door code. Close door firmly, check latch—dC mid-cycle may mean weak door switch.
meaning: dC means the door circuit opened while the dryer was running—Samsung stops heat and drum for safety when the switch reports open.
causes: Door not fully latched
Weak latch allowing vibration to break contact
Broken door switch actuator
User opening door before cooldown on some models
fixes: Push door until single latch click
Inspect strike and latch for wear
Avoid opening during early heat segment
Power off two minutes if dC flashes at idle incorrectly
when_to_call_technician: Replace door switch if dC shows with door firmly closed.

title: Samsung Dryer CL9 Error Code
meta_description: Samsung dryer CL9 comm error. Reset breaker five minutes; CL9 every start needs board service.
meaning: CL9 indicates lost communication between main control and a sub-board—often the display or moisture sensor module on Samsung vented and heat-pump dryers.
causes: Loose ribbon after moving dryer
Power surge
Moisture on UI connector
Failed sub-PCB
fixes: Unplug five minutes or flip 30A breaker
Ensure cord fully seated at terminal block
Keep exhaust vent clear to prevent overheating console area
Note if CL9 at power-on only
when_to_call_technician: CL9 persistent after reset needs main or UI board replacement.

title: Samsung Dryer H01 Error Code
summary: Dryer held in delay start mode
meta_description: Samsung H01 means delay start is active—not a fault. Cancel delay or wait for scheduled start time.
meaning: H01 displays when Delay Start is programmed—the dryer will not begin until the countdown finishes. It is not a heating or vent failure.
causes: Delay Start set accidentally from control panel
SmartThings scheduled start enabled
Button held during power-on triggering delay
Power outage restoring delay state
fixes: Press Delay Start or Time button until H01 clears
Check SmartThings app for scheduled cycles
Hold Power three seconds to cancel pending delay
Start a Timed Dry immediately to confirm normal operation
when_to_call_technician: No service needed unless buttons cannot cancel H01—then UI board fault.

title: Samsung Dryer d90 Error Code
meta_description: Samsung d90 vent blockage warning. Clean lint filter, hose, and exterior cap—fire risk if ignored.
meaning: d90 is Samsung's vent restriction alert—roughly 90% airflow loss detected. Heat may cut back and clothes stay damp after long cycles.
causes: Lint in wall duct beyond the hose
Exterior cap stuck closed
Crushed flexible vent behind dryer
Long duct run with multiple elbows
fixes: Clean lint screen and housing slot
Disconnect rear hose and vacuum both ends
Check exterior airflow while dryer runs
Snake wall duct or hire vent cleaning if weak outside airflow
when_to_call_technician: Strong airflow at disconnected hose with d90 means internal blower housing clog.

title: Samsung Dryer 3C Error Code
meta_description: Samsung dryer 3C motor error. Reset power, check drum turns freely—3C on empty load needs motor service.
meaning: 3C on Samsung dryers means the drive motor or inverter did not achieve commanded speed—drum may not turn or stops mid-cycle.
causes: Overloaded drum with wet heavy load
Belt off pulley jamming motor
Failed motor or inverter board
Loose motor harness
fixes: Unplug 15 minutes
Rotate drum by hand when off
Reduce load weight on retry
Listen for hum without rotation at start
when_to_call_technician: 3C with free drum after reset needs motor or inverter replacement.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine Sd Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool Sd or 5d means suds lock. Use HE detergent, run a rinse cycle, and clean the pump filter if foam blocks drain.
meaning: Sd (also shown as 5d on some displays) is Suds Detected—a suds lock fault. The washer sensed too much foam during drain and extended the pump time trying to break suds. Whirlpool pauses the cycle until suds drop or you intervene.
causes: Non-HE detergent or more than the recommended amount creating foam
Liquid fabric softener poured into the detergent tray instead of the softener cup
Cold water wash with detergent that needs warmer water to dissolve
Partially blocked drain path trapping sudsy water in the tub
fixes: Cancel the cycle and run Rinse & Spin with no detergent on an empty drum
Switch to HE detergent and use the marked lines inside the dispenser drawer
Add one cup of white vinegar to an empty hot rinse to cut residual suds
Clean the pump filter if foam was visible and Sd appeared near the end of drain
when_to_call_technician: Sd on every cycle with correct HE detergent and an empty tub suggests drain pump or pressure sensor service—not a suds issue.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F5E3 Error Code
summary: Door failed to unlock after cycle
meta_description: Whirlpool F5E3 is door unlock fault. Drain water, wait two minutes, power-cycle—replace lock if door stays latched.
meaning: F5E3 means the door lock motor tried to release but the latch never reported unlocked. Whirlpool front-loaders block the door when water remains or when the lock motor fails on the release stroke.
causes: Water still in the tub preventing seal pressure release
Door lock motor failed on unlock after years of cycles
Broken latch hook keeping the lock jammed mechanically
Wiring fault at the door lock harness near the hinge
fixes: Wait two minutes without pulling the door—allow the auto-unlock retry
Drain via the lower emergency hose if water is visible through the door glass
Unplug five minutes, restore power, and press Start/Pause once to trigger unlock
If the door opens after drain but F5E3 returns every finish, schedule lock assembly replacement
when_to_call_technician: Door physically locked with an empty drum after power reset needs door lock motor or main control board service.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F9E1 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool washer F9E1 means long drain. Clean pump filter, check drain hose routing, and reduce suds from excess detergent.
meaning: F9E1 is Whirlpool's long-drain fault—the drain pump ran longer than about eight minutes without emptying the tub. The washer stops before spin and may display F9E1 alongside a flashing Start light.
causes: Pump filter clogged with coins, hair, or bobby pins
Drain hose kinked behind the washer or inserted too deep into the standpipe
Excess suds from non-HE detergent slowing water removal
Failed drain pump motor humming without moving water
fixes: Unplug five minutes, open the lower front panel, and drain the emergency hose into a shallow pan
Twist out the pump clean-out filter, rinse debris, and reinstall firmly
Straighten the drain hose and confirm free flow at the standpipe during Spin
Run Rinse & Spin with half the usual HE detergent if suds were visible before F9E1
when_to_call_technician: Pump hums during drain but water never moves—the impeller or pump motor likely needs replacement.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F8E1 Error Code
summary: Water level stuck on fill or drain step
meta_description: Whirlpool F8E1 means cycle stuck—water level not changing. Check pressure hose, drain path, and inlet valves.
meaning: F8E1 reports the main control detected no water level change during a fill or drain step—the pressure switch reading stayed flat when the board expected rise or fall. Whirlpool halts the cycle to avoid flooding or dry pump run.
causes: Pressure switch air dome hose disconnected from the tub
Drain pump unable to remove water during a drain-phase check
Inlet valve stuck closed so fill never starts
Pressure switch stuck in one position from suds in the air dome
fixes: Inspect the clear pressure hose from the tub dome to the control panel for kinks or loose ends
Run Drain & Spin and listen for pump activity—water should leave the tub
Confirm both supply valves are open and water enters on a short fill test
Power-cycle two minutes after reseating the pressure hose and retry a Normal cycle
when_to_call_technician: F8E1 with confirmed fill and drain at the hose but flat level reading needs pressure switch or main control board diagnosis.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F5E2 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool F5E2 is door lock fault on close. Clear gasket obstructions, check door tab, replace latch if F5E2 repeats.
meaning: F5E2 means the door lock motor could not achieve a locked position after multiple attempts. Whirlpool will not fill or spin until the latch confirms closure—you may hear repeated lock buzzing before F5E2 displays.
causes: Clothing trapped in the door gasket fold preventing full closure
Broken plastic tab on the door edge that inserts into the lock
Misaligned strike plate on the cabinet frame
Failed door lock motor or wiring at the DLS harness
fixes: Remove fabric from the gasket fold and press the door until a single lock click is heard
Inspect the door tab for cracks—replace the door assembly if the hook is broken
Power-cycle by unplugging two minutes, then close firmly and restart
Listen at the lock during start—buzzing with no click points to a failed latch motor
when_to_call_technician: Door closes flush but F5E2 appears every cycle—the door lock assembly needs replacement.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F3E1 Error Code
summary: Pressure switch out of range at idle
meta_description: Whirlpool F3E1 means pressure switch fault. Check air dome hose, clear suds from the dome, test switch if tub is empty.
meaning: F3E1 indicates the water level pressure switch reported an out-of-range reading when the tub should be empty—or the signal never matched expected fill steps. The control cannot safely open inlet valves without valid level feedback.
causes: Air dome hose disconnected, cracked, or clogged with detergent residue
Pressure switch stuck closed from moisture in the hose
Suds in the pressure dome fooling the switch during drain
Failed pressure switch or main control board input circuit
fixes: Unplug and inspect the clear hose from the tub dome to the control panel—reconnect if loose
Blow through the disconnected hose gently—it should be open with no back pressure when the tub is empty
Run an empty Drain & Spin to clear suds before retesting F3E1
Power-cycle and start a cycle with an empty drum to see if F3E1 appears before fill
when_to_call_technician: Empty tub with a clear hose and persistent F3E1 at startup requires pressure switch or control board replacement.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F7E1 Error Code
summary: Heater did not reach target wash temperature
meta_description: Whirlpool F7E1 is a heater fault. Verify hot water supply, check element wiring, test heater ohms on heated cycles.
meaning: F7E1 means the washer could not raise water temperature as programmed—the tub did not pass the minimum heat threshold within the allowed window on a cycle that calls for hot wash water.
causes: Hot water supply valve closed or delivering only cold at the inlet hoses
Open-circuit heating element inside the stainless tub
Loose wire at the heater terminal block behind the rear panel
Heater relay on the main control board not energizing
fixes: Confirm the home hot faucet is on and delivers warm water at the washer inlet hoses
Unplug and inspect heater wire connections at the rear terminal block
Run a Sanitize or Heavy Duty cycle and note whether water warms in the first forty minutes
Measure heater resistance with a multimeter—it should not read open/infinite
when_to_call_technician: Good hot supply and intact wiring with F7E1 on every heated cycle needs tub heater or control board service.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F8E6 Error Code
summary: Water supply fault during fill attempt
meta_description: Whirlpool F8E6 means water not entering properly. Open both valves, clean inlet screens, straighten hoses, check flood hoses.
meaning: F8E6 is a water supply error—the control opened inlet valves but flow did not meet minimum rate or stopped unexpectedly mid-fill. Whirlpool aborts before agitation to protect the drum and heater from dry operation.
causes: One or both supply faucets closed behind the washer
Inlet hose screens clogged with sediment at the valve connections
Kinked inlet hoses restricting flow below 10 psi
Flood-preventing supply hoses tripped their internal shutoff from high flow
fixes: Verify both hot and cold faucets are fully open and water reaches the inlet hoses
Disconnect hoses at the washer, rinse the metal screens inside each valve, and reconnect
Straighten inlet hoses and remove kinks against the wall
Pause/Cancel twice to clear the display, then restart with both valves confirmed on
when_to_call_technician: Strong flow at disconnected hoses with F8E6 during fill points to inlet valve solenoids or flow meter service.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine LF Error Code
summary: Fill cycle took too long to complete
meta_description: Whirlpool LF means long fill. Open supply valves, clean inlet screens, check pressure, and verify both hot and cold connected.
meaning: LF is Long Fill—the washer did not reach the expected water level within the fill timeout, usually about six to eight minutes. Both hot and cold must be connected on Duet front-loaders; single-hose installs trigger LF.
causes: Low household water pressure during peak use elsewhere in the home
One inlet valve coil failed while the other side still trickles
Partially blocked inlet screen on hot or cold connection only
Pressure switch slow to respond from clogged air dome hose
fixes: Open both supply valves fully and run water at a nearby fixture to purge air
Disconnect inlet hoses and rinse screens at the washer valve bodies
Avoid running dishwasher or showers on the same branch during a fill test
Run a Normal cycle with an empty drum and watch fill through the door glass
when_to_call_technician: Good pressure at both hose ends with LF on warm fill usually means dual inlet valve replacement.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine E1 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool washer E1 is a main control error. Power-cycle five minutes; recurring E1 needs ACU or UI board diagnosis.
meaning: E1 on Whirlpool washers signals a Main Control fault—the ACU logged an internal error or lost synchronization with a subsystem. The washer may stop mid-cycle and flash E1 or F1E1 depending on display firmware.
causes: Power surge or brief outage corrupting control memory
Loose wire harness at the central control unit connector
Failed motor control unit communication on models with separate MCU
ACU board component failure after moisture exposure
fixes: Unplug the washer for five minutes or flip the breaker off and on
Confirm the outlet delivers stable 120 V—avoid extension cords and shared circuits
Retry a short Spin Only cycle with an empty drum after reset
Note whether E1 appears at startup or only mid-cycle—timing helps service diagnosis
when_to_call_technician: E1 on every power-on after reset requires main control board or MCU replacement with model-matched parts.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F5 Error Code
summary: Door switch circuit open at cycle start
meta_description: Whirlpool F5 means door not detected closed. Check latch, strike alignment, door switch continuity before replacing parts.
meaning: F5 is a door switch fault—the control did not see the door circuit close within the startup window. On some Cabrio and VMT top-loaders F5 relates to lid lock; on front-load Duet models it maps to door latch circuits similar to F22/F5E2.
causes: Door or lid not fully closed against the strike
Broken lid lock tab on top-load models
Failed door or lid switch not clicking when latched
Loose wire at the latch harness entering the door or top panel
fixes: Close the door or lid firmly until a single click is heard—do not slam
Inspect the strike and latch tab for cracks or misalignment
Power-cycle two minutes and retry Start without opening mid-cycle
On top-loaders, listen for lid lock engagement before the basket tries to spin
when_to_call_technician: F5 every cycle with a door that appears closed needs latch switch or lock assembly replacement.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F6E8 Error Code
summary: UI lost communication with main control
meta_description: Whirlpool F6E8 means display board cannot talk to ACU. Check UI harness, reseat connectors, replace UI if F6E8 persists.
meaning: F6E8 reports lost communication between the User Interface board and the Appliance Control Unit. Buttons may be dead, lights flash randomly, or the display shows F6E8 while the washer will not start.
causes: Loose ribbon cable between UI and main control behind the console
Corroded connector pins after steam from a leaking fill hose
Failed UI board after power surge
Main ACU UART port failure preventing handshake
fixes: Unplug five minutes for a full communication reset
Remove the top or rear console access per model and reseat the UI-to-ACU harness
Confirm no moisture on connectors from a prior fill hose leak at the back
Retry power-on—if F6E8 flashes immediately with no button response, stop cycling breakers
when_to_call_technician: Reseated harness with instant F6E8 at boot needs UI board or ACU replacement—order by full model number.

title: Whirlpool Dishwasher F8E4 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool dishwasher F8E4 means dispenser did not dispense. Clear clogged dispenser, check wax motor, verify detergent type.
meaning: F8E4 means the control opened the detergent dispenser but did not detect the expected water flow or actuator movement through the dispenser chamber. Wash performance drops because main wash detergent never entered the tub.
causes: Caked detergent blocking the dispenser lid or wax motor plunger
Failed wax motor or dispenser actuator not opening the door
Using tablet pods that stick to a wet dispenser cup
Loose wire at the dispenser harness on the door liner
fixes: Remove caked detergent from the dispenser cup and lid with warm water and a soft brush
Run a cycle and listen for the dispenser click during the first wash fill
Use powder or liquid detergent until F8E4 clears—some pods delay release
Wipe the dispenser cup dry before adding detergent on the next load
when_to_call_technician: Clean dispenser with no click during wash fill requires wax motor or dispenser assembly replacement on the door.

title: Whirlpool Dishwasher F6E1 Error Code
summary: Vent and fan circuit electrical fault
meta_description: Whirlpool dishwasher F6E1 means vent fan error. Check vent on door top, inspect fan wiring, test fan motor if F6E1 returns.
meaning: F6E1 is a vent/electric fault on Whirlpool dishwashers with a drying fan in the door vent assembly—the control detected an open or shorted circuit when energizing the vent fan during the dry phase.
causes: Vent fan motor seized from steam mineral buildup
Loose connector at the vent assembly on the inner door top
Damaged vent door linkage preventing fan startup
Control board relay failure on the vent fan circuit
fixes: Inspect the top vent grille on the door for blockage from grease or plastic film
Open the inner door panel and confirm the vent fan spins freely by hand
Reseat the vent fan harness plug at the door liner
Run a High Temp wash and note whether F6E1 appears only at the dry end of the cycle
when_to_call_technician: Fan blade free with F6E1 every dry phase needs vent fan motor or vent assembly replacement.

title: Whirlpool Dishwasher E1F9 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool E1F9 means thermistor fault. Check OWI sensor harness at tub, replace sensor if E1F9 persists after reset.
meaning: E1F9 reports the wash temperature sensor (thermistor in the tub or OWI assembly) sent resistance outside the expected range. The control cannot regulate heat or verify sanitizing temperature without valid readings.
causes: Thermistor wire broken at the tub clamp near the heating element
Connector corrosion after steam exposure at the lower tub harness
Shorted sensor from water in the OWI optical housing on some models
Control board input circuit failure reading the thermistor
fixes: Disconnect power and inspect the lower tub area for loose thermistor plugs
Wipe the OWI window if your model uses an optical water sensor alongside thermistor
Power-cycle one minute and run a Normal wash—note if E1F9 appears before heat step
Avoid running until E1F9 clears if water temperature on display reads erratic
when_to_call_technician: Technicians ohm the wash thermistor at room temperature against Whirlpool spec—replace if open or out of range.

title: Whirlpool Dishwasher E1F1 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool E1F1 means OWI sensor error. Clean OWI window in tub floor, check harness, replace OWI if E1F1 continues.
meaning: E1F1 flags the Optical Water Indicator—the sensor in the tub floor that detects water presence and turbidity. Whirlpool uses OWI feedback for fill verification and soil sensing; E1F1 means the signal was missing or out of range.
causes: Detergent film or grease coating the OWI lens in the tub sump
Loose OWI harness connector under the tub after prior service
Cracked OWI housing allowing water into the sensor cavity
Failed OWI sensor board after repeated high-temp wash cycles
fixes: Cancel the cycle and wipe the OWI window in the tub floor with a soft cloth and vinegar
Remove the lower rack and inspect the round lens near the sump—no scratches or cracks
Power-cycle two minutes after cleaning and run an empty rinse cycle
Avoid abrasive cleaners on the OWI lens—they scratch the optical surface
when_to_call_technician: Clean lens with E1F1 at every fill attempt needs OWI sensor assembly replacement under the tub.

title: Whirlpool Dishwasher FAE4 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool FAE4 is dispenser actuator fault. Inspect detergent cup, test wax motor, check door wiring for FAE4.
meaning: FAE4 means the detergent dispenser actuator did not reach the expected position after the control sent open command—related to F8E4 but specifically on models that monitor actuator travel with a position switch.
causes: Wax motor plunger stuck from hardened detergent buildup
Broken dispenser door spring not allowing full open travel
Position switch on the dispenser not actuated when door opens
Door harness wire broken at the hinge from repeated door flexing
fixes: Soak the dispenser cup with hot water and clear hardened detergent from the wax motor area
Manually flip the dispenser lid—it should spring back and latch smoothly
Run a cycle and watch the dispenser during first wash fill for full door swing
Use liquid or powder detergent until FAE4 clears to rule out pod sticking
when_to_call_technician: Dispenser door moves freely by hand but FAE4 every cycle needs dispenser assembly with wax motor replacement.

title: Whirlpool Dryer F4E4 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool dryer F4E4 means lint screen thermistor fault. Clean screen housing, reseat sensor plug, replace if F4E4 returns.
meaning: F4E4 reports the lint screen thermistor—mounted in the lint filter housing—sent an out-of-range temperature signal. Whirlpool uses this sensor on some ventless and console models to detect restricted airflow at the filter slot.
causes: Lint packed around the thermistor pocket in the filter housing
Thermistor connector loose inside the top console or bulkhead
Open thermistor after overheating from running without the lint screen installed
Main control board input failure on the lint screen sensor circuit
fixes: Remove and wash the lint screen, then vacuum the housing slot where the screen seats
Confirm the lint screen is fully inserted—running without it triggers false readings
Unplug and reseat the thermistor harness at the filter housing if accessible per service guide
Run a timed dry ten minutes and note whether F4E4 appears at startup or mid-cycle
when_to_call_technician: Clean housing with F4E4 at every start needs lint screen thermistor or control board service.

title: Whirlpool Oven F1 Error Code
summary: Electronic control memory or relay fault
meta_description: Whirlpool oven F1 means control board fault. Power-cycle breaker five minutes—persistent F1 needs ERC replacement.
meaning: F1 on Whirlpool ovens indicates an Electronic Range Control fault—bad EEPROM checksum, stuck relay, or internal board failure. The display may beep and show F1 at idle or lock out bake and broil until power is removed.
causes: Power surge corrupting control board memory
Welded bake or broil relay on the ERC
Moisture on the control board from steam or spill down the panel
Age-related component failure on the oven ERC
fixes: Flip the oven breaker off for five minutes—not just Cancel on the panel
Verify the range cord or junction box connections are tight and not overheating
After restore, observe whether F1 appears before pressing any key
Avoid using the oven for bake or broil until F1 is cleared or diagnosed
when_to_call_technician: F1 immediately after breaker reset requires Electronic Range Control replacement—model-specific part required.

title: Whirlpool Oven F2 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool oven F2 means runaway heat. Turn off breaker, check stuck relay, test oven sensor—do not use oven until F2 clears.
meaning: F2 means the oven temperature sensor reported cavity temperature above the safe limit—often above 620°F on electric models—or the control detected a runaway heat condition. Whirlpool locks out heat to prevent fire risk.
causes: Stuck closed bake or broil relay continuing heat after shutoff command
Shorted oven temperature sensor wiring touching hot metal
Failed ERC reading sensor voltage incorrectly
Oven cooling fan not running after broil leaving residual over-temp reading
fixes: Turn off the oven breaker immediately and allow the cavity to cool completely
Do not open the door repeatedly during cool-down—wait at least thirty minutes
After cool, restore power and check whether F2 returns without starting a cycle
If F2 appeared during self-clean, wait for full cool-down before any reset attempt
when_to_call_technician: F2 with a cool oven after hour-long power off needs oven sensor, relay board, or ERC service before next use.

title: Whirlpool Oven F1E0 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool F1E0 means EEPROM fault on oven ERC. Power reset; if F1E0 returns at boot, replace electronic control board.
meaning: F1E0 is the modern F/E display for EEPROM failure on the oven Electronic Range Control—the non-volatile memory that stores calibration and cycle data failed checksum at power-on.
causes: Power surge during storm or grid fluctuation
Partially tripped breaker dropping one leg of 240 V supply
Corrupted firmware after interrupted self-clean power loss
Failed ERC memory chip after years of heat exposure behind the panel
fixes: Flip the dedicated oven breaker fully off for five minutes
Confirm the range outlet delivers 240 V on both legs to the terminal block
Restore power and note whether F1E0 appears before touching any pad
Record the full code pair if additional digits flash after F1E0
when_to_call_technician: F1E0 on every power-on after breaker reset requires ERC replacement—board is not user-serviceable.

title: Whirlpool Oven F9E0 Error Code
summary: Cooling fan not operating when required
meta_description: Whirlpool oven F9E0 means cooling fan fault. Check fan behind control panel, test harness—F9E0 after broil needs fan service.
meaning: F9E0 reports the oven cooling fan did not run when the control commanded it—typically after broil or self-clean when the control panel and electronics need forced air cooling. Whirlpool locks functions until the fan circuit is verified.
causes: Cooling fan motor seized from grease dust behind the control panel
Loose fan connector at the ERC or fan motor pigtail
Failed fan motor capacitor on models with separate cap
ERC relay not sending voltage to the cooling fan circuit
fixes: Power off the breaker and allow the oven to cool if F9E0 appeared after broil
Listen for cooling fan hum behind the top panel after restoring power at idle
Confirm no object blocking the fan grille above the oven or in the control console
Retry broil briefly—F9E0 within two minutes of broil end confirms fan circuit fault
when_to_call_technician: No fan spin with voltage at the motor needs cooling fan motor or ERC relay replacement.

title: Whirlpool Oven F6E1 Error Code
summary: Door lock circuit fault during clean or bake
meta_description: Whirlpool oven F6E1 means door switch error. Check door closure, latch motor, and switch harness on wall ovens.
meaning: F6E1 on Whirlpool ovens indicates a door switch or latch circuit fault—the control did not see the expected door lock position during self-clean, broil vent lock, or standard bake interlock checks.
causes: Door not fully closed against the gasket before latch attempt
Failed door latch motor on self-clean lock assembly
Broken door switch actuator on the latch striker
Loose wire at the door switch harness through the hinge area
fixes: Close the oven door firmly until the latch engages—listen for a click on lock models
Cancel any active clean cycle and wait for latch release if the door is locked
Inspect the latch hook and striker for bent metal preventing switch actuation
Power-cycle the breaker five minutes after confirming the door opens and closes freely
when_to_call_technician: Door closes properly with F6E1 every start needs door latch motor or door switch assembly replacement.

title: Whirlpool Washer 5d Error Code
summary: Display shows 5d instead of Sd—suds lockout
meta_description: Whirlpool 5d is the same as Sd suds detected. Use HE detergent only, run Rinse & Spin, clean pump filter if needed.
meaning: 5d appears on some Whirlpool LED panels where the letter S looks like the numeral 5—it is the same Suds Detected lockout as Sd, not a separate hardware fault. The machine extended drain trying to break foam before spin.
causes: Regular detergent oversudsing in an HE washer
Softener or detergent in the wrong dispenser compartment
Heavy soil cycle with double detergent dose
Drain path slow enough that suds linger in the tub
fixes: Run Rinse & Spin with an empty drum and no soap
Use HE detergent to the lowest line for soft water
Add one cup white vinegar on a hot rinse if foam remains visible
Clean the pump filter if 5d appeared at the end of drain with water left
when_to_call_technician: 5d every wash with correct HE dose and clean filter suggests pressure sensor or drain pump service—not suds.
